text: TLDR Founders 2023-05-12 Generative AI impact 💥, venture debt dries up 🏜️, co-founder match tips 🤝 📈 Headlines & Trends Venture Debt Is Drying Up (5 minute read) Following the collapse of SVB, venture debt fell to $3.5B in Q1, the lowest level since 2017, a sign that more startups may be in for financial peril. Generative AI: An Asteroid Impact Event on B2B Software (5 minute read) We are all talking about AI but this is different from the iPhone moment that happened for mobile. Adapting quickly and embracing AI across newcos and existing companies will be critical to winning in the end. US Investment In China Tech Scene Falls As Political Headwinds Strengthen (3 minute read) Sharing because this is potentially a harbinger of other economic dominoes set to fall globally. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How Vanta Grew Through The Downturn (17 minute read) A deep-dive into how Vanta continued to grow throughout the bear market, with particular focus given to the timing of its Series B raise, its expansion overseas, the building of new moats, and the scaling of its team. Addressing Misaligned Incentives (8 minute read) Deb Liu discusses how misaligned incentives can cause problems within organizations. She suggests that to ensure successful outcomes, companies need to identify and correct any incentive structures that don't align with their overarching goals. By setting clear objectives and aligning incentives, organizations can foster an environment where teams are encouraged to work towards shared objectives rather than pursuing conflicting interests. She emphasizes that leaders must be proactive in recognizing and addressing such misalignments before they negatively impact the company's performance. Alma’s Path To Product-Market Fit (10 minute read) The article shares the story of Harry Ritter and the development of his telehealth startup, Alma. Ritter, being a non-technical founder, had to pivot multiple times to find product-market fit. His approach included performing meticulous customer research, engaging in numerous conversations, and adjusting his model based on feedback. He also leveraged his domain expertise in healthcare. Despite early setbacks, Ritter's persistence and adaptability eventually paid off, establishing Alma as a successful telehealth platform. ⚒️ Tools & Resources How to Know if It’s a Good Co-Founder Match (4 minute read) Finding a co-founder is actually more involved and has greater implications than actual marriage. This is not an exaggeration. StarChat (HuggingFace Space) An AI-powered helpful coding assistant. Zmo.ai (Product) An AI tool to create realistic backgrounds for your product photos. Think of it like your own personal green screen. 🎁 Miscellaneous How I Invented A Grill Gun And Did $2.5M in Sales Since Our Launch in 2020 (6 minute read) I love a good story of product/market fit + timing. I also think these are just plain cool and like the idea of a product niche that can still be a profitable business. What Does Productivity Mean? (5 minute read) The piece explores the challenge of defining and measuring productivity in the context of remote work. It argues that traditional metrics may not accurately reflect productivity in a remote setting and highlights the importance of considering employee well-being and work-life balance. The article also discusses how management approaches need to adapt to remote work environments. This Is NOT Your Value Prop (4 minute read) Kyle Poyar emphasizes the importance of value proposition in business. He points out that companies often misinterpret their value proposition as merely their product or service, without considering the broader context of the problem they are solving for customers. Poyar advises businesses to differentiate between features, advantages, and benefits, to better articulate their value proposition. He also emphasizes the need for companies to continuously validate and refine their value proposition to stay competitive. ⚡️ Quick Links How To Do Outbound (7 minute read) Sam Blond breaks down how to properly do outbound at your startup. The Art Of The Doomer (7 minute read) In Kyla Scanlon's piece, she discusses the concept of "Doomerism" and its implications. The term "Doomer" is used to refer to individuals who have a pessimistic view of the future, often based on environmental, political, or societal concerns. She elaborates on how "Doomer" content can inadvertently lead to inaction and despair, and suggests that while it's essential to stay informed, it's also crucial to maintain a balanced perspective and focus on possible solutions to the challenges we face. Estimated IQ Distribution Of Children Given IQ Of Parents (4 minute read) The article delves into the complex relationship between the IQ of parents and their children. It elaborates on the concept of regression to the mean, explaining how children of parents with extreme IQs (either high or low) tend to have IQs closer to the average. The piece also discusses how factors such as genetics and environment play a role in determining a child's IQ. It concludes that while parents' IQ can give a general idea of the child's potential IQ range, it is not a definitive predictor due to the numerous variables involved. 5 Interesting Learnings From HubSpot (3 minute read) Jason Lemkin breaks down 5 things to learn from HubSpot, including focusing on efficiency, slowly increasing ACV, and hiring slowly and smartly. title: TLDR Founders 2023-07-05
text: TLDR Founders 2023-07-05 AI echo chamber 🤖, guide to founder-led sales 💰, the GOAT of SaaS metrics 🐐 ) TLDR is hiring a part-time curator to join our TLDR Founders newsletter curation team. The ideal candidate would be a current or former startup founder, preferably someone who has raised venture capital, enjoys keeping up with the latest trends in the startup ecosystem, and generally spends a lot of time hanging out with other founders. Time commitment is roughly 6 hours/week (Sunday, Tuesday, Thursday). To apply, please send your LinkedIn or resume to jobs@tldr.tech along with a couple sentences on why you'd be a good fit! 📈 Headlines & Trends What the European AI Act Means for You, AI Developer (11 minute read) The EU AI Act is the world's first legislation on artificial intelligence, similar to GDPR but for AI. It introduces definitions for 'foundation models' and 'general-purpose AI systems' and sets out dos and don'ts for AI practices. The Act also focuses on trustworthy AI development and includes obligations for high-risk AI systems. The legislation is currently going through final negotiations before enforcement. Are People In Tech In An AI Echo Chamber? (HN Thread) Everyone in tech knows AI is revolutionary. But do people outside of tech feel the same? This Hacker News thread dives into that question and whether the AI hype has gotten out of hand. Why Transformative AI Is Really, Really Hard To Achieve (21 minute read) While humans have a good track record of innovation, the trend growth rate of GDP per capita has never exceeded three percent per year. There has yet to be a technology that breaks the three percent threshold. Only strong evidence should cause us to expect anything different. AI is likely transformative in the same way that the internet was, but it has many hurdles in the way to the accelerating growth rates predicted by some. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Building Prestige (6 minute read) A playbook to manufacture personal prestige with a small amount of high-quality content. 5 Interesting Learnings From SEMRush (5 minute read) A compilation of things to learn from SEMRush, the newest public SaaS with $290M in APR. The Guide To Founder-Led Sales (4 minute read) A guide for founders to crush each step of the sales process, from lead gen to close. ⚒️ Tools & Resources HiveSpark (Product) HiveSpark is an AI-powered content generator for startups, providing task templates for various business needs. bohr.io (Product) bohr.io is a platform for developers to deploy their applications easily and for startups to hire developers for cost-effective digital product development. Withpublic (Product) Introducing Withpublic.com: an MVP marketplace to buy, sell, or request free MVPs. Find co-founders and unlock opportunities by exchanging failed MVPs for better ideas. 🎁 Miscellaneous Intellectual Laziness (20 minute read) The article discusses the concept of intellectual laziness in organizations. It argues that intellectual laziness is the tendency of organizations to "juke the stats" and lie to themselves instead of diagnosing and solving root problems. The Return to Office Mandate is Destroying Job Satisfaction (5 minute read) The title says it all and the numbers don’t lie. Workers are begrudgingly complying as companies move to push workers back into the office, while looking out for other opportunities and side-hustles. Building Minimum Lovable Products (67 minute listen) Jiaono Zhang breaks down why we should be building “minimum lovable products” rather than minimum viable products. ⚡️ Quick Links What Gets To The Front Page Of Hacker News? (4 minute read) An exploration into what kind of content lands on the coveted front page of Hacker News. Meta Threads To Launch July 6th (3 minute read) Meta’s Twitter competitor, Threads, is expected to launch on July 6th. The GOAT Of SaaS Metrics (5 minute read) CJ Gustafson makes the case that revenue per employee is the most important metric to evaluate a company by as it cuts through all the noise. Listen to your customers, build a product that truly serves their needs, and acknowledge that your product likely needs to evolve as their needs change, particularly if you’re building for startups that grow up one day. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Llama 2 - Meta AI (Product) AI continues to move at breakneck speed. Meta is pushing forward with the latest iteration of their Llama open source model trained on 2 trillion tokens. Reforge Artifacts (2 minute read) Reforge just launched Artifacts - a library of documents capturing lessons and insights from established founders and operators. Wope (Product) SEO meets AI, helping you better understand the competitive landscape in a post-generative-AI world. 🎁 Miscellaneous How ICs can get recognition for their work on big projects (27 minute audio) An interview with Dr. Cat Hicks, Director of Pluralsight Flow’s Developer Success Lab, where they double-click on how to ensure good work is seen in larger organizations. Companies requiring employees to be full-time in-office are struggling to recruit new talent (5 minute read) There seems to be an inverse correlation between the number of mandated days in the office and a company's ability to attract and retain talent. Less flexibility causes churn - who’d have thought? Zack Schildhorn, Founder of Series Z, gives advice on developing a successful pitch for investors (34 minute audio) Khosla Ventures just dropped a collection of recordings from their CEO Summit in May 2023. In this recording, Zach talks about how successful fundraising is all about provoking genuine human emotions through storytelling. ⚡️ Quick Links How to be assertive: Propose Solutions (1 minute video) Whether it’s a romantic date, strategy whiteboard session or a valuable new partnership, learning to propose new ideas effectively can create momentum in your conversations and better position you as a leader. Surviving a slowdown (4 minute read) We know this message has been drilled in a lot over the last year, but Sean offers some simple mental models to apply when faced with uncertainty and slow market conditions. How Much Equity Should I Give A Key Startup Advisor? (1 minute read) Finding the right advisor is hard, incentivising them accordingly even more so. How do you build skin in the game at the right level and reward experienced advisors for their time? Here’s a “back of the napkin” kinda way. title: TLDR Founders 2023-07-21
text: TLDR Founders 2023-07-21 Shakeup at Sequoia 🤠, a16z: the future of gaming 👾, space tech funding down 🧑‍🚀 📈 Headlines & Trends Space tech funding comes back to earth (3 minute read) Less venture funding dollars are going to space tech startups. In 2021, $12.1 billion went to space tech across 450 deals. In 2022, this decreased by 25% to $9 billion. So far in 2023, we’ve only seen $2.4 billion go to them. To the moon, maybe, maybe not. Michael Moritz closes a long chapter at Sequoia Capital, and four other partners depart the firm (7 minute read) Five partners are leaving the firm, but the headline is that Mike Moritz is officially moving on after 38 years service. As the venture capital scene gets more competitive, it’ll be interesting to see what 2024 and beyond has in store for Sequoia. ChatGPT's performance is slipping, apparently (4 minute read) UC Berkeley researchers found that ChatGPT has not improved over time, and in fact, may have gotten worse. The researchers suggest that one of the reasons for this decline could be the result of new safety protocols being implemented by OpenAI. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Your guide to SaaS product metrics (8 minute read) This article explores the metrics you should be aware of as you build out your product-led growth muscle at your SaaS company. It double-clicks on the funnel conversions you can expect from when a visitor lands on your website and eventually becomes a paying customer. Building an an Early Stage Startup: Lessons from Akita Software (11 minute read) Gergely, writer at The Pragmatic Engineer, interviews Jean Yang, the founder of Akita, which was very recently acquired by Postman. They dive into finding your co-founder, fundraising, hiring, pivoting, and all the challenges faced along the way. Effective > Productive (1 minute read) Optimizing for productivity and being busy doesn’t necessarily mean you’re working on the right things. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Microsoft and Meta expand their AI partnership with Llama 2 on Azure and Windows (Product) MSFT is on a tear as of late and they are playing every angle they can. Llama 2 was just announced and Microsoft is going to be offering it up on Azure and Windows. If you’re an early-stage company on Azure you get the best of all worlds. SaaS Boilerplate (Product) This keeps coming across my feed and given we’re in the post-AI era, being able to spin up a SaaS backend quickly and customize your  AI-tools du jour is no joke. GitHub Copilot Chat beta now available for every organization (Product) Putting the power of GPT-4 into the hands of developers at a conversational level is going to be yet another game changer. AI continues to move at the pace of … well … AI. 🎁 Miscellaneous a16z - the neverending game: how AI will create a new category of games (20 minute read) AI has long played a role in games, from procedurally generated environments, to machine learning and NPC behavior. Generative AI is the next frontier in game development, enabling hyper personalization and adaptive environments. How about a game that designs itself and never ends? Rich and Anonymous (5 minute read) An insightful read that digs into the positive correlation between wealth and social liability. In today’s society, the wealthier you get, the more you feel a responsibility to give back to family, friends, and the global community. Find Your Winning Idea By Selling Products That Don’t Exist Yet (7 minute read) The story of Material Security’s path to product-market fit, in which they took a circuitous route where they tested multiple different ideas. ⚡️ Quick Links Seth Godin on goals and expectations (1 minute read) As a founder, you want to get things done fast and see results now. Sometimes this isn’t always possible. Godin provides a nice reminder that we have to be persistently patient. 5 Interesting Learnings from Datadog at ~$2 Billion in ARR (3 minute read) File this under “problems we’d like to have” - thinking through being worth 18x ARR with continuing massive growth across every KPI. Fascinating read and something to aspire to. There’s No Rebound In Sight For Unprofitable SaaS (2 minute read) The SaaS industry is facing a profitability crisis, with many companies spending more money on sales and marketing than they earn in revenue. title: TLDR Founders 2023-07-24
text: TLDR Founders 2023-07-24 Incoming Twitter rebrand 🐦, Block sues Visa & Mastercard ⚖, AI website generator 💻 📈 Headlines & Trends Twitter’s brand might soon be taking flight (2 minute read) Elon says he’s looking to change Twitter’s logo, tweeting, “And soon we shall bid adieu to the Twitter brand and, gradually, all the birds”. He likes the letter “X”, apparently. Block Sues Visa and Mastercard Over Interchange Fees (1 minute read) How much inflation is caused by the hidden and unreasonable fees charged by networks? Square thinks it’s too much, so they filed an antitrust lawsuit on Friday with the U.S. District Court. McKinsey weighs in on GenAI through a collaboration with Cohere (4 minute read) McKinsey and Cohere will help organizations integrate generative AI into their operations, redefine business processes, train and upskill workforces, and use this emerging technology to tackle some of the toughest current challenges. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Want To Steal a Customer from the Competition? You Gotta Do The Work (2 minute read) Whether it's for payments, your CRM, or data warehouse, switching providers is a pain. If you’re looking to win over a new user, instead of telling them why you’re better than their current provider, you’re better off rolling up your sleeves and helping them do the migration work. Accelerate The Growth of Your Subscription Business with Usage Based Pricing (7 minute read) With the rise of AI, it’s becoming increasingly easy to lean into usage-based pricing. Consumers and businesses are getting used to this model although it is always a balancing act of too-much-too-early. This article covers several tactics to use when exploring the usage-based model in conjunction with subscription pricing. Copy what’s popular and differentiate where it matters (13 minute read) The web is increasingly becoming less text heavy and more visual. Furniture in your Airbnb looks the same and iconic brand logos are starting to look similar. What’s happening? Paras argues that conformity in core domains brings trust, and all founders should be thinking about this for their startups. ⚒️ Tools & Resources A library of reading materials for the wannabe angel investor (Airtable Database) Angel investing as a founder can be a great way to embed yourself more deeply into the startup ecosystem, build a network, and acquire knowledge that might help you tackle your own startup challenges. Wix announces a new AI site generator (2 minute video) The company’s new AI Site Generator tool will let Wix users describe their intent and generate a website complete with a homepage, inner pages, text, and images. Talk about leverage. 93% of You Say Competitors Lie To Close Deals. You’ve Gotta At Least Get Good at Counter-FUD (5 minute read) FUD and Counter-FUD are going to be a part of the SaaS game no matter what. Get your game on point with some poignant tips from Jason Lemkin. 🎁 Miscellaneous What to watch in AI (30 minute read) Mario from The Generalist gives a breakdown on some of the potential use cases of AI and thirteen companies you may not have heard of which are leading the way. Paving the Path for “Blue-Collar AI” Professionals (5 minute read) AI might be closing the doors for a few workers, but it could open some for others. Myra Roldan explores what blue collar work 2.0 might look like in the age of AI, from AI Farmhands to Warehouse Operators. State of Fintech Q2 2023 (4 minute read) Here are the key takeaways from CB Insights latest report, which explores the state of global fintech. Global funding falls, LatAm and the Caribbean funding doubles, all five of the quarters IPOs come from fintechs based outside of the US. ⚡️ Quick Links Does your customer success team need to know your product? (2 minute read) CS teams are focused on ensuring your customers have a good time. But do they need to know your product intimately? A poll revealed that most think yes, but some leaders and CS managers think no. Really, it’s about ensuring minimum-viable-trust between your users and their point of contact on the CS team, and this involves being able to talk about their product problems. Timing is hard (3 minute read) Team, tech, and timing are the elements to getting “lucky” in tech. This article talks about the hardest part: timing. The Power of No to Amplify the Impact of Yes (4 minute read) A short, succinct post about the power of NO. In a world where everything is happening at once, thinking through what is most important is key. title: TLDR Founders 2023-07-26
text: TLDR Founders 2023-07-26 Rise of 3-person Unicorns 🦄, historic lessons in funding 💰, think other buckets 🪣 Drata is the leader in security and compliance automation — find out why (Sponsor) Drata is making the rounds. The company has pioneered a new approach to collecting evidence and achieving continuous compliance with SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and other frameworks — potentially saving hundreds of audit-prepping hours. And the cherry on top: Drata automatically matches your risks with pre-mapped controls to unlock the power of automated tests. Thousands of customers trust Drata, including companies such as BambooHR, Notion, and Postman. 447 reviewers on G2 have rated its solution 4.9 on average. Want to find out what all the fuss is about? Now’s your chance. TLDR readers and community members get 10% off and waived implementation fees. Claim your exclusive offer now 📈 Headlines & Trends The Pilgrims Raised 4 Rounds of Funding (2 minute read) If you don’t know your near-term history about technology over the last 50 years, you really should dig in. History always repeats itself and we’re headed into another massive boom cycle. What lessons can you take from the past to apply today? Can you read the tea leaves? Twitter in hot water with the “X” rebrand (1 minute read) The US has over 900 trademarks for the letter “X”, including those held by Microsoft and Meta. Bold move Elon, let’s see how this one plays out. The 3-person Unicorn Startup (6 minute read) The countdown is on and the signs are already there: the first 3 person company with $100M+ in revenue has already been founded and is on its way to unicorn status. I cannot imagine building a non-AI-powered company ever again. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics What happens when tasks fall outside of your organizational structure? (4 minute read) No matter how well you lay out your organizational structure and processes, there’ll be many tasks and events that don’t fit well. It will be unclear who is responsible for handling it or how it should be handled. In this article, Sean Byrnes gives some insight on a few tactics to handle these outlier tasks. R&D is the key to unlocking free-cash-flow in SaaS (6 min read) Single product software eventually reaches market saturation, hitting the top of the s-curve and causing growth to slowly grind to a halt. The antidote is continuous investment in research and development, facilitating an evolution into multi-product greatness. 20 Ways Tech Leaders And Teams Can Make The Most Of Summer Slowdowns (7 minute read) I love the notion of a Summer slowdown in startups. Of course, if you’re fundraising, or all of your buyers are on holiday, things can slow down. But ultimately, there’s still an endless laundry list of work to be done. As a founder, you want to continue momentum towards these goals, but think about how your team is taking the time to pause, reflect, and reset over Summer. ⚒️ Tools & Resources What got you here isn’t getting you there (12 minute read) An insightful chat with Art Levy, CSO for Brex, on the ups-and-downs of leading a company, and more importantly, about how you have to keep hacking/evolving your offerings if you expect your company to grow. 12 tips for networking as an introvert (5 minute read) There’s this divide in startups. Some advocate strongly for networking events and connecting with people in general. Others focus on ruthlessly building your product, seeing networking events as a waste of time. There’s likely some truth in both sides of the argument. Not all networking events are equal. If you do decide to go, here’s some thoughts on how to show up effectively. ChatGPT just launched their Android app (1 minute read) OpenAI’s ChatGPT is now available in the Google Play store for Android devices. Now you can ask your fundamental business questions on the go. 🎁 Miscellaneous Don’t get addicted to fantasy economics (6 minute read) We’re all talking about how the era of growth at all costs is over. Startups can no longer build a strategy around using VC funding to subsidize a service in order to acquire market share and later figure out how to monetise effectively. But, what if this play looked a bit less like Uber and Doordash, and more like OpenAI? Would it be sensible for a model to subsidize their service in order to optimize their access to training data in order to outpace competition through product refinement? How Shopify builds product (12 minute read) Lenny sits down with Glen Coates, VP of Product at Shopify, to understand how they’re still growing fast and shipping products at an impressively startuppy rate, despite being 17 years old and nearly 10,000 employees deep. Start new employees in the deep end (2 minute read) Hiring is a gamble - you never really know what a new employee is capable of. As a founder, your role is to test the boundaries of their capabilities, and the way to do that is to put them up to bigger and bigger tasks that encourage them to be their best. Take more chances with your new hires. ⚡️ Quick Links Payments infra has an enormous geopolitical impact (1 minute read) From the launch of FedNow in the US and growing RTP hype to CBDCs and ongoing sanctions,  payments are hot. Alarming Decline in Startup Creation Presents Challenges And Opportunities (4 minute read) The numbers don’t lie: the number of new startups, and investment in startups, is WAY down for 2023. However, opportunities abound. title: TLDR Founders 2023-07-28
text: TLDR Founders 2023-07-28 Sequoia pulling back on crypto 🔙, personal finance robots 🤖, European biotech fund raises $400M 😯 📈 Headlines & Trends Everything is Cyclical (5 minute read) In the dog days of Summer, you can’t help but take stock of things and look back. History has a way of repeating itself and everything is cyclical. We’re in another rerun of a disruptive tech cycle. Now is your chance to maximize. Fed lifts rates, Powell leaves door open to another hike in September (4 minute read) We’d be remiss if we didn’t bring your attention to the latest rate hike, and more importantly, the specter of future increases. Some say we’re bouncing off this recession, with markets still hot and inflation not cooling. All of these factors affect your company’s ability to scale and raise money. Sequoia’s pulling back on crypto (2 minute read) Sequoia Capital has cut the size of its cryptocurrency fund by more than 65%, from $585 million to $200 million. Talk about a pivot. We assume this is all going into AI, right? 🧠 Strategies & Tactics My Favorite Interview Question (3 minute read) I like to ask my team if the person they are recommending to hire would pass the “airport test” - if you were stuck in an airport for the next 8 hours, would you be psyched to chat with this person? I like Matt Blumberg’s approach as well though. Raising Capital in H2 2023: What You Really Need To Know with Creandum Partner Peter Specht (7 minute read) It isn’t the heady days of 2021 (“shut up and take my money!”), but there are glimmers of hope out there. This exhaustive review of current data on each stage of investing is a must read if you’re headed out to raise this Fall. Raising Prices For Your Product: Should You Do It? If So, How? (6 minute read) With just about every major SaaS provider raising their prices, should you take the plunge? Quick read on the things you’ll want to think through before doing so, as well as tips on how to test and roll it out. ⚒️ Tools & Resources From five to 50: How to hire at your early-stage startup (5 minute read) It never hurts to study up on early-stage hiring especially in a post-AI, post-pandemic world. This is a great read that breaks down the 5-to-50 hiring tricks of the trade. Pricing plans of 50 bottom up SaaS companies (Airtable database) If you’re thinking about how to price for your SaaS startup, looking around at other examples in the market can be helpful. Here’s an Airtable database categorizing 50 different SaaS pricing plans. Building a second brain using AI (7 minute read) I’m an active Notion user, largely for curating interesting reads, infographics, and thoughts. In this article, Azeem Azhar unpacks how you can use AI to level up your research skills and turn Notion into a valuable database where you can store knowledge and more easily spot thematic connections and patterns. 🎁 Miscellaneous a16z explores whether AI will unlock personal finance management (4 minute read) We’re increasingly seeing more people want to take control of their finances. Fees charged by financial advisors, funds, and wealth managers, and valuable leaps in money tech, are encouraging people to go it alone when it comes to personal finance. Is there an opportunity for AI to take the wheel on this trend? New Research on Embracing and Inspiring the Gen Z Workforce (4 min read) Not all workers are created equal and your approach to Gen Z could be benefited by reviewing the recap of this Deloitte research. Dear SaaStr: What Happens To Employees When a Company Gets Acquired? (2 minute read) You never know, but the formula is pretty clear if/when you get acquired. Jason from SaaStr walks us through what to expect. ⚡️ Quick Links Don’t Do It For The Money (5 minute read) Marc Randolph lays out why he thinks founders shouldn’t be focused on “getting rich” but instead should be focused on solving hard problems with amazing people. What do you want to spend your time on really once your basic needs are met? 16 Lessons I Learned From Studying the $700M Brand Liquid Death (8 minute read) Canned water? C’mon. You wouldn’t think of canned water as a massive business, but Mike Cessario leveraged some (what are now) obvious tricks to bootstrap his now ubiquitous canned water brand. European biotech fund raises $400m (2 minute read) Within all the AI hype, it’s nice to see biotech getting a spotlight. text: TLDR Founders 2023-07-31 SaaS to boom in 2024 🚀, dev productivity barometer 📉, paradox of AI and data roles 🤖 📈 Headlines & Trends Signs We’ve Touched The Bottom (3 minute read) Tomasz Tonguz believes we’ve hit the bottom for cloud revenue as the demand for AI is stabilizing infrastructure spend. The Most Valuable Tech Companies Still Aren’t Buying Startups (3 minute read) 2023 is on pace to have the smallest number of acquisitions by the Big Five (Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Nvidia) in years, with these companies making just five known startup acquisitions so far. The Second Half Of 2024 Could Be Really Good For SaaS (3 minute read) Things are looking up for SaaS companies, so much so that Jason Lemkin believes a flood of strong SaaS IPOs are on the way for the second half of 2024. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How To Build A Successful Consumer Subscription Business (8 minute read) An exploration into how to overcome the systemic challenges of B2C subscriptions and build a strong business. Latest benchmarks on the journey from Seed to Series A in Europe (2 minute read) Non-founders love setting benchmarks for founders. If you’re a European founder, or simply curious about the ecosystem, this article breaks down the average amounts raised before Series A and the ideal time frames between each successive raise. How to measure and improve developer productivity (1h16m listen) The average developer spends 17 hours per week on maintenance issues like debugging and refactoring. What happens when you reduce that time down significantly, enabling them to allocate more time to building the product? How much new opportunity and revenue could that time unlock? ⚒️ Tools & Resources Bleakers (Product) Bleakers is a single entry space for product development. Supademo (Product) Communicating how products work is critical but it’s done inefficiently. Supademo makes this process easier by helping anyone create intuitive click-through demos. AI Logo Art (Product) AI logo art transforms your logos into stunning art with AI. 🎁 Miscellaneous The Paradox Of AI And Data Roles (2 minute read) Tomasz Tunguz explains why even though AI will automate some data work, it will also demand much more from data teams. The strength of weak ties (7 minute read) In 1973, Stanford sociologist Mark Granovetter showed just how important casual acquaintances are. 50 years on, this article is still a nice refresher on the power of maintaining your network beyond close friends and family. Being in Command rather than in Control (5 minute read) As a high growth founder, if you feel like you’re in control, you’re likely not moving fast enough. Being in command is a different dynamic. Command means you know what to measure and what you need to do, and although it might not all be progressing in the right direction, you have a grasp. ⚡️ Quick Links Selling AI Products Around The World Is Still Too Hard (5 minute read) Although building a company has never been easier, it is still very difficult to sell products globally. a16z explains why Merchant of Record programs might be the solution. Apple Puts Restrictive Rules Around Their Vision Pro Dev Kits (2 minute read) Some developers have drawn attention to the terms and conditions for the Vision Pro developer kit, which are notably restrictive and specific. The Importance Of Timing Your Next Raise (4 minute read) Eric Feng dives into why founders should think about and time their next round of fundraising. Trying to do everything all at once almost always leads to doing nothing. Winning the embedded fintech race is all about distribution (6 minute read) In this article, Matt Brown unpacks how embedded fintech is a positive sum game for those involved in the value chain. Servant leader, not order taker (2 minute read) Successful leadership is about creating the conditions for success. ⚡️ Quick Links Choose your customers (2 minute read) We’ve all seen the Pareto principle applied to customers. You want to avoid the noisy, low-willingness to pay and demanding customers. Seth Godin encourages everyone to think about the customer type they really want to delight. title: TLDR Founders 2023-08-04
text: TLDR Founders 2023-08-04 $400M in AI + carbon funding 🪵, great Chief People Officer 🤗, managing priorities & energy 🧘🏽 📈 Headlines & Trends AI + Carbon = Funded Startup (4 minute read) AI and carbon removal are two of the hottest trends in tech right now. Startups that combine the two have raised more than $400M in the last two years, with carbon tracking software an especially popular focus. Where Will AI Have The Biggest Impact? Healthcare (5 minute read) Healthcare is 20% of the US economy, but the industry has been slow to adopt technology. With AI, healthtech companies no longer need to fight the uphill battle of training people on software. Instead, they can sell AI that acts like a person and takes more and more of the work off healthcare professionals’ plates, enabling them to work on more interesting problems and practice at the top of their licenses. Apple Card’s savings account reaches $10B in deposits (3 minute read) Apple launched a savings account in partnership with Goldman in April 2023, and it’s already reached $10B in deposits. This showcases the power of brand trust, distribution, and ease-of-use in gaining critical mass with new ventures. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics What Does Great Look Like In A Chief People Officer? (3 minute read) Three great tips from serial entrepreneur Matt Blumberg about what you should look for in a great people officer. Guide to all the incoming top tech conferences (6 minute read) Planning a tech conference roadshow? Here’s your list of all the major tech conferences coming up across the US and Europe. Managing your priorities and energy (11 minute read) “Company, Team, Self” is a useful decision-making framework. However, the glue that binds all of this into outcomes is energy. By over-relying on this framework, you often neglect yourself or focus on things that don’t give you energy as a founder or operator. This has a finite runway - you'll eventually have to factor in doing things that give you energy to ensure you can keep on doing things sustainably. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Freepik Image Generator (Product) Freepik allows you to generate your own unique AI images directly in Figma. Vizly (Product) Vizly uses AI to analyze your data in seconds. Reflex (Product) Reflex enables you to build web apps in pure Python and deploy them with a single command. 🎁 Miscellaneous WTF Happened In 2023? (12 minute read) With recent advancements in AI, superconductors, nuclear energy, and infrastructure, 2023 will likely be looked back on as the year that technological progress really began taking off again. The Future of Longevity: Keeping Healthy People Healthy (9 minute read) The real opportunity in healthcare is not just curing those that are ill - it’s about keeping healthy people healthy for longer. This thoughtful piece about the opportunity for TechBio founders to prevent rather than treat is a long but fantastic one. Cultivating A State Of Mind Where New Ideas Are Born (17 minute read) Many founders in the early 2010s thought that working within a thriving social scene of peers would help them percolate ideas as they built their startup, but there are no famous success stories from people who tried. Working in solitude allows for serious work to be done. Being in an environment where the opinions of others matter can kill ideas quickly. This essay analyzes the working notes of several highly creative individuals to distill different approaches to developing a creative state. ⚡️ Quick Links America Has A Problem. Often this is a proxy for the investor simply not being sold on you yet. Use this junction as an opportunity to ask specifically what terrible things about startups in this space the VC is trying to avoid and feed that into your deck for the next pitch. To PLG or not to PLG (8 minute read) Product-led growth means that your product has the ability to acquire, activate, engage, or monetize without human intervention. For B2B, it’s not a question of whether you should invest in product-led growth, but rather when and how. This article provides some useful frameworks to consider when setting out on this journey. ⚒️ Tools & Resources OpenCoPilot (GitHub Repo) An open source AI copilot for your SaaS product. This takes time, and you’ll need to get comfortable with making a lot of mistakes along the way. (Sponsor) Many software leaders still see security as a hassle, a box-ticking exercise, or a resource sink. But things are changing fast. It shows that building startups and pursuing innovation is all about experimenting and taking shots on goal. Failures aren’t failures, simply attempts. You need a co-founder (4 minute read) Although there’s been some successful solo founders, you’ll often hear that you need a co-founder in a complimentary domain in order to build a truly great startup. But what is a co-founder? It’s really just a title, someone you trust to run the company in your absence. It doesn’t need to be someone with equal equity as you. The Engineering executive’s role in hiring (24 minute read) A comprehensive dive into the components of an effective hiring process for technical roles. Build a scalable system, train your hiring managers, set benchmarks and reporting windows with your recruiting team, and determine compensation levels before you kick off the hiring process. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Poozle (GitHub Repo) Poozle is an open-source single API for Product Integrations. Stay close to your users. How to get someone on your podcast (2 minute read) Make it as easy as humanly possible for them to say yes, schedule, do the prep, and show up. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-13
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-13 Square’s outage 💳, the pitfalls after $10m ARR 🦺, culture’s 50-day story 📃 5 steps to turn your security posture into a competitive advantage (Sponsor) Trust is a critical part of any deal. Customers, big and small, need to know how you’ll protect their data before they can do business with you. If you’re not prepared, this can lead to lengthy questionnaires, long security reviews, and awkward sales conversations. Use this five-step checklist for busy executives to learn key practices that help position security as one of your strengths : ☑️ Using security to close more deals, faster. ☑️ Streamlining security requests and automating manual security processes. ☑️ Extending trust to third parties with vendor risk management. Get the checklist from Vanta 📈 Headlines & Trends Square’s outage (1 minute read) Payments company Square had an outage on Thursday last week. This happens of course, but the impact on businesses that use Square to take payments can be huge. The event added fuel to the ongoing culture war around migrating to a cashless society. Confidence vs Arrogance (4 minute read) As a founder, you have to be confident in what you’re doing. That’s how you sell people on the vision to invest in you, work with you, buy from you. However, you need to understand the boundary between confidence and arrogance. Regulating artificial intelligence (17 minute read) Slowing the pace of innovation allows competitor countries to catch up. Letting innovation run unfettered can unlock a Pandora's Box of problems. This article spins an interesting story that showcases why regulating artificial intelligence is so difficult. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Integration strategy (7 minute read) As you scale, the number of integrations you have with providers is going to increase. Your CRM, Finance stack, ERP, and collaboration tools all need to talk to each other. Interoperability between applications is a continuous strategic decision for founders. You need to have an owner of this strategy in your startup. When to borrow debt (2 minute read) Selling equity in your startup is the most expensive form of financing. Debt is cheaper, but only accessible under certain circumstances, like when you know your unit economics, have product market fit, and have proven repeatable channels to find customers. The top 10 mistakes founders make after $10m ARR (9 minute read) From stepping away from founder-led sales completely to hiring mistakes. Here’s a list of some things you can be mindful of as a founder of a scaling startup that’s in the growth phase. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Trickle (Product) Trickle produces AI-generated insightful summaries for each uploaded screenshot. Buildshare (Product) Generate product updates for Twitter when you ship new code. AI Score My Site (Product) Find out if your website can be discovered and ranked by AI-powered search engines and what you can do to improve it. 🎁 Miscellaneous If you want to trick investors, tell them your CAAR (5 minute read) Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) is a staple metric of investor expectations. Contracted Annual Recurring Revenue (CAAR) is being used more by founders to show unrealized revenue from committed contracts. It bumps the numbers, but it might leave you in hot water further down the line. How AI can spark both technological and business model innovation (2 minute read) Truly great innovation comes in pairs. Technology innovation and business model innovation. They work together and deliver outlier results. AI is the next technology innovation, but have we found its counterpart on the business model side yet? Securing AI is the Next Big Platform Opportunity (4 minute read) When it comes to AI and security, there is a significant amount of work and effort required. This article breaks down the risks and challenges and looks at the opportunities for companies in this new world order. ⚡️ Quick Links Working remotely doesn’t always mean from home (1 minute read) Remote can be anywhere that inspires you to work at your best. Say/Do ratios (2 minute read) “Under-promise and over-deliver” - how many times have we heard that? The mantra is focused on ensuring you only take on enough work that you can really do justice. But are you in danger of being seen as a slacker? There’s a balance somewhere. Company Culture is the last 50 days (2 minute read) The incorrigible Jason Fried of 37 Signals gives his take on culture and how to view it around the things that have happened around a 50-day rolling window. Best quote: “Culture is the nonfiction story of an organization. It writes itself.” Get our free, 5-minute newsletter read by 200,000 startup founders, entrepreneurs, and CEOs Subscribe Join 360,000 readers for one daily email Privacy Careers Advertise

title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-15
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-15 Conversation with Sam Altman 🗣️, Xbox and embedded finance 🎮, Swan raises $40M 💰 📈 Headlines & Trends Swan’s $40m raise (2 minute read) Despite global fintech funding being down 84% YoY, French fintech Swan bags $40m to scale their embedded banking services across Europe. Hope for fintech and Europe? FTX gets approval to sell its crypto assets (2 minute read) FTX has been given approval to liquidate its assets in order to begin paying back its users in USD. However, it still has a lot of assets. How will this liquidation impact the crypto market as supply floods the market? Xbox introduces Embedded Finance in Gaming (2 minute read) Xbox has launched the Xbox Mastercard in collaboration with Barclays. The Xbox Mastercard is a rewards card that players can use to earn points through everyday purchases to redeem on games. Wasn’t it Angela Stange who said in 2019 that every company will be a fintech company? 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Signs your Chief People Officer isn’t scaling (3 minute read) If you have a Chief People Officer who is overly focused on the transactional parts of their operations, like payroll and benefits, doesn’t speak up in executive team meetings, and has morale or turnover issues in their own team, you’ve got big problems. How I CEO: On Taking Advice As A Leader (4 minute read) Leadership is challenging, but getting advice from experienced peers and investors makes the job much easier. The key to getting the most out of advice is considering the situational context around the advice and maintaining enough humility to properly receive the advice. Picking Sharp Problems, Increasing Virality, And Unique Product Frameworks (76 minute audio) A discussion on why you need to find the “sharpest” problems, how to operationalize continuous customer discovery, tactical strategies for making your products more viral, and more. ⚒️ Tools & Resources TimeOS (Product) Your personalized AI for meeting notes and scheduling. Solidroad (Product) A flight simulator for Sales conversations. Empower your reps to practice at their own pace, refine their approach, and receive tailored feedback. ChartGenAI (Product) Generate any chart you want using a single sentence. 🎁 Miscellaneous In conversation with Sam Altman (24 minute video) Azeem from Exponential View sits down with Sam Altman during his latest world tour to talk about AI, regulation, and his thoughts on the future of OpenAI. Reverse engineering your ideal life (3 minute read) As founders, we can often get lost in the to-do’s. The details of these tasks consume us. Here’s a nice reminder to reflect on the real goals of life, which often don’t involve money. It’s about the people you retain and live with along the way. The importance of founder pedigree in emerging technologies (7 minute read) This is a fascinating read that argues how investors behave around new technologies. Without benchmarks and historical insight, investors will revert back to founder-market-fit, using signals like pedigree and education. A canonical example of this is the amount of researchers and PhD wielders that have been recipients of the latest AI funding boom. ⚡️ Quick Links Elements Of Exceptional Startups (8 minute read) All exceptional startups have some things in common: an exceptional CEO, executive team, investors, talent, and product. How you endure it matters most (2 minute read) A healthy reminder that as a founder you’re going to be banging your head against the wall almost daily. How you handle the bumps and continue on course matters. The most important skill (1 minute read) Qui-Gon Jinn nailed it, your focus determines your reality. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-18
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-18 AI job concerns 🤖, SoftBank's autonomous trucking bet 🚚, sales response time ⏳ 📈 Headlines & Trends Three in four Americans believe AI will reduce jobs (3 minute read) Most Americans think artificial intelligence will negatively affect the U.S. job market, with 75% saying it will decrease the total number of jobs over the next 10 years. Bosses are back in charge (5 minute read) Between RTO, AI, and all the layoffs in tech, it feels like power in the workplace has shifted away from the employees and back to employers. SoftBank makes a billion-dollar bet on autonomous trucking startup (2 minute read) This is a big shift for SoftBank, who in recent years has been moving towards doing smaller and more selective deals. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to take time off and use it well (11 minute read) Jumping directly into a new job or a startup idea straight after another is a bit like dating immediately after a breakup. You don’t want to fall into the “rebound” idea or job. Taking time off requires planning your finances and setting self-expectations. How to be a better sales executive (2 minute read) Whether you’re a founder doing sales or a sales rep, it seems we’ve collectively gotten a little bit slower at promptly responding to customers, follow-ups, and inbound interest. The best way to keep someone engaged is to not keep them waiting. 5 interesting learnings from Samsara at almost $1bn ARR (2 minute read) Growing 40% YoY between 2022 and 2023 and cash flow positive, Samsara is back to growth. There are several stats in this article, but perhaps the standout is how Samsara compensated its sales team on net new revenue, rather than new logos, allowing them to upsell into their existing user base or seek out new business. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Kick (Product) Kick provides daily bookkeeping for the modern business owner. OneAudio (Product) Transform voice recordings into clean summarized notes. FeatureCat (Product) Collect more feedback, plan the right features, and build better products. 🎁 Miscellaneous Is fintech a house of cards? (9 minute read) The rosy macroeconomic conditions of the 2010s have certainly come to an end. The relationship between fixed interchange fees, which often form the staple of fintech revenue, and unfixed increasing interest rates is putting pressure on newly minted technology companies. Why venture capitalists will overpay for seed rounds (3 minute read) The startup ecosystem has experienced a tumultuous period, with the transition from a hyper-growth market to one that rewards a different style of operation. While overall valuations in the seed stage have remained relatively unchanged, the top decile of startups has been less impacted than the second decile, largely due to the involvement of better-known firms who are happy to pay more for a spot on the cap table. How to Become an Angel Investor (7 minute read) Most people think that angel investing is for the ultra-wealthy. As it turns out, the vast majority of angels are investing $1k-$5k into startups. This in-depth post provides tactical tips for how to get started. ⚡️ Quick Links Saying the hard thing when it matters (1 minute read) We often avoid having tough conversations or calling out the things in meetings we really should be. Here’s a reminder that it’s okay to feel uncomfortable or fearful doing it. Learn how to do it anyway. The Ad Industry Bailed on News. Can An AI Solution Offer A Way Back? (3 minute read) AI is looking to take on several dying markets and news is just another avenue. This article looks at how AI might be a path forward for news. We’re already seeing this happen with smaller local TV affiliates with some success. JPM to launch payroll to compete with Square (1 minute read) Partnering with SF-based Gusto, JPM is looking to offer digital payroll processing in order to appeal to small business customers. It’s nice to see an incumbent taking meaningful actions towards innovation. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-20
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-20 Instacart's IPO move 🛒, subscription vs. usage debate ⚖️, embracing the startup journey 🚀 TOMORROW: Compliance automation in practice — join the Q&A session (Sponsor) Obtaining security standards for your software used to take 400 hours of manual work and basically blocked everything else for a month or two. Today, you have tools like Vanta , which automates 90% of the work. Join the live Q&A session to learn how you can tackle that big compliance project before EoY . The Vanta team will be answering your questions about SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, and other frameworks, and will also show a practical demo covering real-life compliance scenarios. Last chance to join! 📈 Headlines & Trends Instacart prices shares at $30 as IPO market warms up (1 minute read) Instacart plans to sell 8% of its stock, which could bring in around $660 million, in order to fuel demand for online grocery shopping post-pandemic. Santander commits to €100 million fund for Spanish startups (1 minute read) Santander committed €67m to a new €100m fund in an effort to help boost Spain’s entrepreneurship ecosystem. It’s always cool seeing large global banks invest in their roots. My Reflections on my summer in India’s Silicon Valley (7 minute read) In this article, Ashu Garg, General Partner at Foundational Capital, compares Bangalore’s emerging SaaS scene to Silicon Valley, predicting that by 2030, the enterprise software industry will revolve around two poles—Silicon Valley and Bangalore. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Can we even afford to do SMB sales in the U.S. anymore? (3 minute read) Inflation up, salaries up, marketing costs up, CAC on the rise. In response to higher expected OTE from sales reps, some startups are moving their SMB sales teams outside of the US. Subscription vs Usage Based pricing models (8 minute read) A fascinating read that breaks down the pros and cons of usage-based versus subscription pricing. More founders are leaning into usage-based pricing as a way to better align their revenue with the value a user extracts from their product. How SaaS business models are changing (5 minute read) The market is tough right now and it's impacting how founders grow their SaaS startups. We’re seeing product-led growth tactics surge, headcount-based growth being less rewarded, pricing changes being used as a strategic lever, and increased focus on reducing cloud costs. ⚒️ Tools & Resources CX Assist (Product) Personalized, AI-powered email responses to boost efficiency, nurture clients, and transform your sales and support customer experience. DialMe (Product) A voice-enabled AI interviewer built to get users talking and you learning. Klu AI (Product) Unify your data so that you can seamlessly search and discover across apps and files. 🎁 Miscellaneous Fighting against regulatory capture for a better future (16 minute read) Regulation tends to slow things down and make innovation more expensive. To overcome regulatory barriers, startups need to be both well-resourced and well organized. Regulations are a set of rules and you need to know them in order to play the game. Now is the time for innovation hubs like Silicon Valley to get closer to and work with Washington DC. A Golden Age Of Building? (29 minute read) An informative read of lessons from people achieving great things quickly, for example, the Empire State Building, the Pentagon, Skunk Works, and SpaceX. AI Regulation from Elad Gil (13 minute read) This article unpacks the challenges with regulation, including how it can stifle innovation, slow progress, and ultimately drive these innovators to build their technology in alternative countries where restrictions may not be as…strict. ⚡️ Quick Links The future is uncertain, live immediately (1 minute read) Who knows what’s around the corner. Enjoy the good times, plan for the worst, but just make sure you’re living. The Only Way To Guarantee Startup Success (5 minute read) Instead of focusing solely on the outcome, remember to enjoy the journey as well. Related to Instacart, this is cool (1 minute read) A nice reminder that serendipity works in mysterious ways, and venture capital is a long game. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-22
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-22 Wild west of e-commerce 🤠, boards that are helpful 🎬, how to avoid doing fraud 👮 📈 Headlines & Trends Musk, Gates, and Zuckerberg walk into an AI meeting (1 minute read) Sounds like the start of a bad joke. Tech titans Elon Musk, Bill Gates, and Mark Zuckerberg met in Washington on Wednesday to talk about AI regulation. They were joined by founders from leading AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI. Hopefully, we hear some insight from the meeting soon! Software transcends borders—why not money? (5 minute read) Global money movement is a really complex topic. It’s really a lot like language. Each country grew up with its own system and way of communicating with money, and we’re still in the phase of translating that into a global language. The wild west of e-commerce (10 minute read) In light of Klaviyo’s S-1 filing, this article dives into the e-commerce space and the infrastructure that runs it. Much of the plumbing behind e-commerce is invisible to the consumer. Amazon’s FBA, Klaviyo’s marketing, Shopify storefronts, and wholesale marketplaces like Faire all enable the gears of e-commerce to turn without the consumer knowing about it. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to measure what marketing activities are actually driving revenue (17 minute read) A comprehensive read covering how to map your funnel, set up tooling, and start tracking the right data from marketing activities at your B2B startup. Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky on why he's deep in the product details (60 minute podcast) The bigger the company gets, the more likely it is that the founder or CEO doesn’t spend time with their customers. Brian Chesky has a refreshing take on how a CEO should always maintain a high degree of customer-centricity and product knowledge. Helpful boards (8 minute read) Board meetings can often be a huge waste of time. A board member should be your confidant. They should tell hard truths and play the long game. Here’s a helpful breakdown of the signals that indicate whether you’ve got a good board or not. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Capsule (Product) The AI-powered video editor for content and marketing teams. Cardinal (Product) An AI-powered backlog enriching your features with customer feedback and revenue data. Shortwave (Product) The AI executive assistant you’ve been looking for. 🎁 Miscellaneous How to avoid doing fraud (4 minute read) The most important rule of business is to never lie about your metrics. False metrics quickly spiral out of control, and you’ll begin to layer on fraud after fraud to keep the lie alive. Before you know it, the only thing that exists is the lie you’ve told and not the company behind it. Generative AI’s Act Two (15 minute read) Generative AI’s first act of developing foundational models and novelty apps is coming to an end. In its place comes Act Two: where generative AI solves human problems end-to-end. Failing better (4 minute read) Startups fail all the time, but not all of them fail gracefully. The core things to think about if you need to close shop include your team, your accounts payable, and your customers. Make sure you preserve the relationship with your team, don’t screw small businesses you owe money to, and don’t kick customers who may rely on your product or service to the curb. ⚡️ Quick Links The car that was allergic to vanilla ice cream (1 minute read) Customer feedback can sometimes bamboozle you. How on earth did they encounter that problem? Oftentimes it’s a case of getting to the root cause, which might have nothing to do with how the problem was first presented to you by your user. a16z’s interactive global payments page (1 minute read) Not really a 1 minute read, but a fun and interactive page from a16z that breaks down the global payment and fintech network, highlighting some of the key players across the globe. Automate (But Automate Last) (2 minute read) Before you go automating things, make sure all unnecessary elements are removed first. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-25
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-25 Dual-role CEOs 🧠, $1B for climate tech companies ☁️, upward organizational communication 📢 📈 Headlines & Trends HSBC makes $1bn available for climate tech companies (1 minute read) Last week we flagged that Santandar was committing £100m to Spanish startups, now HSBC is weighing in on climate. It's looking to support climate companies that are focusing on EV charging, battery storage, sustainable food and agriculture, and carbon removal technologies. The rise of offline apps (3 minute read) Sales of “dumb phones” are on the rise, the count of apps in Apple’s App Store and Google’s Play Store are down 25%. As the appetite for tangible experiences grows, companies are popping up that help facilitate IRL interactions in order to get people away from their phones. Dual Threat CEOs that Build and Invest (2 minute read) While a tad bit controversial, being a dual-threat CEO can be really handy. Understanding deals, talking with entrepreneurs, and seeing what is happening through deal flow is actually really beneficial for a CEO who is usually too in the mix of their own company. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Communicating upwards in your organization (10 minute read) “Don’t bring me problems, bring me solutions”. We all know it, but rarely do you hear it being said in an organization. Effective communication with your leadership is about acknowledging they have limited cognitive bandwidth and acting accordingly. If you’re at the top, this is a nice reminder to have empathy over what’s happening below. Things to keep in mind when preparing a product launch (4 minute read) Time spent on UX tinkering should be proportional to the level of conviction you have on your hypothesis. Have a launch checklist and allocate an overall owner for each one. Be patient - launches often take longer than forecast. The worst outcome is a mediocre success (4 minute read) If you test a hypothesis and it fails, good, you can now try something new. If it only kinda works, you’re in this limbo state of deciding whether to double down and re-test or pull the plug. This is where time and resources go to die. Defining success and failure specifically and leaving little room for the inbetween is vital. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Docera (Product) An AI-powered tool that simplifies document creation for personal and professional use. BulkCorrector (Product) Never deal with ChatGPT’s character limit again - BulkCorrector segments your text into manageable pieces for bulk corrections. Journalist Hunt (Product) A database of more than 340,000 journalists to feature your business. 🎁 Miscellaneous How to drive word of mouth (1+ hour podcast) Goodwill, NPS, mindshare - we often used these words to explain the ambient value created in the ecosystem when people simply love our product. But can you really architect and measure the value of word of mouth? The CPO of Wise thinks so. When it’s over: thoughts on selling your company (4 minute read) Going through an exit, whether positive or negative, is an emotional experience. You’re selling something you’ve likely poured years into, and it’ll never be the same again. Deciding when, and for how much, often comes down to trusting your gut. Vertical operating systems and embedded fintech (8 minute read) We’re witnessing the convergence of SaaS and embedded finance. SaaS platforms are introducing financial services into their existing offerings, increasing their user LTV through stickiness, and unlocking new revenue streams. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech funding in London down (1 minute read) According to data compiled by KPMG, investment dropped to £4.6 billion in the first half of 2023 compared to the £10.8 billion witnessed in 2022. Is it better to target a small market or a large one? (1 minute read) Peter Thiel (billionaire venture capitalist and co-founder of PayPal and Palantir) describes his framework for evaluating markets. Start small and expand. Is Generative AI an Enterprise Thing? (2 minute read) The dust is settling around the excitement around generative AI, which means people are settling in to figure out how it can be useful in enterprise. Short story: it’s hugely beneficial. Long story: it’s going to take time. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-27
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-27 Investment in battery startups 💰, AI for brainstorming business ideas 🤖, 10 vs. 100 person startup 🔟 Board meeting coming up? Get the CTO Deck Template (Sponsor) Many technical leaders struggle to convey their teams' health, impact, and priorities to business stakeholders- leading to misaligned expectations and decision-making. Streamline your next engineering health update with this CTO Board Deck template by LinearB . Start with battle-tested slides that make it easy to highlight the KPIs and key project updates that business leaders care about. You know that engineering is driving business results. Now, you can prove it with data. Download the full deck template 📥 📈 Headlines & Trends Investment in battery startups on the rise (3 minute read) In the last month, we’ve witnessed three $1bn+ investments into battery startups. The space is on trend to surpass 2021 funding levels by the end of the year, which is perhaps the only industry out there to do so. Series, which aims to replace ERP systems, lands $25M (9 minute read) Series is a platform designed to centralize and automate business finance and operations. Backed by $25M in funding, it offers modular ERP-like features, enabling companies to choose functionalities best suited for them. By streamlining operations, it aims to reduce the complexity and cost businesses face when managing multiple vendors and systems. How Is AI Going to Impact The SaaS Landscape Over The Coming Years with a16z GP Kristina Shen (7 minute read) We all know SaaS is going to be impacted by AI, but the question is what is the next wave going to look like? This in-depth discussion with Kristina Shen goes into why it’s more about insights than information and unlocking the real power of AI for SaaS. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How Miro grows: tactical lessons from the $17.5B whiteboard (40 minute read) This is a long but rewarding read that unpacks the nuances of product-led growth, freemium offerings, the differences between onboarding users with single-player and multiplayer products, and how to intelligently think about product-led sales. It’s one for your weekend coffee. Experience Doesn’t Matter (6 minute read) While we might not completely agree, experience can sometimes be overrated when it comes to hires. This article goes into the weeds on why it's more than just experience when it comes to looking at candidates as well as some tactics for focusing on the fundamentals that a potential candidate might have. The biggest difference between running a 10 person startup versus 100 (2 minute read) As a founder, you have to let go. Focus your efforts on hiring great VPs and give them the freedom to make decisions and ultimately their own mistakes. But only let go once you know you have that great VP in place. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Product planning brainstorm template (Figma Template) A template you can use to brainstorm your product roadmap with your team. Vespio (Product) Vespio provides AI-powered sentiment analysis so your sales teams never waste leads. Bestever (Product) Create brilliant marketing visuals in minutes using AI. 🎁 Miscellaneous Thoughts on early-stage partnerships (5 minute read) Partnering with bigger firms for your startup can often seem like a silver bullet to your distribution worries. In reality, there’s absolutely zero substitute for developing your own audience. Lots of big partnerships are just efforts to gather information. How To Use AI to Brainstorm A Billion-Dollar Business Idea (3 minute read) Guest author Dan Kraemer for Crunchbase News goes into his methodology around leveraging AI to brainstorm the next big idea. The generalist disease (5 minute read) The hallmark symptom of people with Generalist Disease is optimizing for optionality. Individuals who pursue good brands to build their resumes, jumping from each every few years and diving into something completely new each time. This is an excellent strategy early in your career - to figure out what you enjoy - but later on, you need to specialize in something. ⚡️ Quick Links Mercedes-Benz partners with Mastercard (2 minute read) Mercedes is getting into payments in collaboration with Mastercard. Starting in Germany, users can now pay for fuel using an in-car fingerprint sensor. Towards stickiness (1 minute read) We often talk about product stickiness like it’s a hack that can be gamified. It’s really about the people who would miss your product if it disappeared. Find them and delight them. The case for not deferring happiness (1 minute read) Most CEOs postpone happiness in order to build their business. However, they can build better, more valuable businesses if they make happiness a priority. title: TLDR Founders 2023-09-29
text: TLDR Founders 2023-09-29 Indian innovation 🏆, how to grow internationally 🌍, inside mind-reading AI 🧠 📈 Headlines & Trends What to watch in India (25 minute read) India has a population of 1.4 billion. By 2041, 59% of the population will be of working age. That’s a huge workforce. The government has been investing in tech, and large VCs are propping up in the region. This deep-dive on eight startups showcases the innovation coming from the Indian ecosystem. AI, Hardware, and Virtual Reality (11 minute read) AI, combined with hardware, is going to enable groundbreaking forms of virtual and augmented reality. Here’s a breakdown of the latest news in each of those domains. Is Data Still A Moat? (2 minute read) Generative AI is reshaping the traditional value placed on vast data collections. The uniqueness and importance of proprietary data may be waning as large language models can be fine-tuned with minimal data and even generate synthetic datasets. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Is your international growth strategy a hot mess? (5 minute read) The prospects of internationalization at your startup are exciting. Bigger TAM, new revenue, risk diversification - but often there’s a misalignment between internal buzz and external traction that’s explained by a lack of preparation. International expansion is a strategy, not a quick tactic. How founders can engage with a Chief People Officer (4 minute read) Regardless of the seniority of the Chief People Officer, have them report to you. This signals that the founder/CEO cares intimately about the needs of employees and keeps you connected to the culture and general vibe. 8 Ways SaaS Companies Can Use AI To Get Ahead (12 minute read) This blog post features several AI SaaS use cases for various departments, highlighting ways the technology can be used to gain a competitive edge. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Strut (Product) Strut is an AI notebook that turns rough notes into finished content. Collato (Product) AI assistant for product teams to find, summarize, and generate new content based on your own product knowledge. App Store Spy (Product) Gain immediate insights into apps, niches, and keywords. 🎁 Miscellaneous Working at a Startup vs in Big Tech (11 minute read) Startups are an excellent environment to learn and build generalist skill sets, but they’re financially risky and stressful. Big Tech enables you to build a specialism and a financial buffer, but you’ll have to deal with bureaucracy and a lack of purpose. Written from an engineer's perspective. Inside mind reading AI (24 minute watch) More startups are popping up to help us read and manipulate our mental states to help us relax, learn, and reduce pain. Neuralink, Mendi, and FocusCalm are just some of these startups. These companies will gather data from users if they are given access to their brains. Who owns this data? What are the biggest problems you face as a startup founder? (3 minute read) Your biggest challenge evolves as the company grows, from finding a co-founder to simply sticking to it and launching new products in order to expand your TAM. ⚡️ Quick Links Artisanal email (1 minute read) AI is making it easier to craft emails. Sales teams are using tools to find leads and outbound them more easily than ever. The signal-to-noise ratio is going to grow. This automated future raises all kinds of questions for businesses and the role crafted emails will play in it. Trust your customers (2 minute read) Default to trusting your customers. The goodwill it brings is worth the loss from bad actors. A simple note taking system (1 minute read) There are many methods for note-taking, from transcribing every call to archiving everything in Notion. Often, the simplest solution scales. Have a doc for daily notes and one for each individual you interact with regularly. title: TLDR Founders 2023-10-02
text: TLDR Founders 2023-10-02 AI and jobs 🤖, what sets great teams apart 🔥, leadership from Starbucks CEO ☕ 📈 Headlines & Trends How companies spy on their employees (3 minute read) Monitoring employees' activity goes beyond just checking who swiped their badge to get into the office. Tools are propping up that enable employers to monitor employee internet usage and even take screen recordings. Fintech loses UK funding crown (2 minute read) The UK’s energy startup sector has raised the most VC cash in 2023, knocking fintech off the top spot it has held for a decade. It has raised $3bn so far this year versus fintech’s $2.1bn, down from a high of $13.5bn in 2021. AI and jobs (1 minute read) AI is great at doing tasks, but not so much doing full jobs. Sam Altman argues that AI will enable humans to focus on different work that AI can’t do. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Life visions and why you probably need one (3 minute read) You likely have OKRs for your business, but do you have them for yourself? We often just wake up each day and try our best. Having an idea of what you’re working towards specifically can help make it feel less like Groundhog Day. What Sets Great Teams Apart (90 minute video) A conversation on what exactly takes a team from simply a good team to a truly great team. What We Learned About Paid Ads For A Dev Tool (7 minute read) A deep dive into how to make your paid ads more effective, especially if your target customers are developers. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Krea Logo Illusions (Product) Turn your logos into amazing visual illusions. Semiform (Product) Replace your forms with AI agents in order to get higher response rates and happier users. Colors AI (Product) Automatically gather, consolidate, and categorize customer feedback to prioritize product development. 🎁 Miscellaneous The business of annual letters (6 minute read) A big part of a CEO's job is to communicate with their investors. Good writing often reflects good thinking. Some of the most successful companies and investors out there are also great writers that publish a yearly letter to their investors. Company Culture is a Crucial Factor for Job Seekers (5 minute read) Written for operators, but useful for founders if you flip it. Your culture should be identifiable throughout your hiring process. Give time for potential hires to interview you and learn about your company culture. Rules for better thinking, how to reduce blindspots, & more (2 hour video) Two podcasting legends, Shane Parrish and Tim Ferris, combine forces to talk about tools, tactics, and mental models for clearer thinking. ⚡️ Quick Links The problem with short term thinking (2 minute read) Being short-termist is like playing Jenga. Each time you cut a corner, you remove a block, and your tower gets a little bit more fragile. Confusion and certainty (1 minute read) As a founder, we don’t want to admit that we don’t get something. If we pretend we’re not confused, the confusion compounds. Feel comfortable admitting you don’t understand something, and often people will help you out. Tips on leadership from Starbucks CEO (1 minute read) Stay close to your product and customers. Execs at Starbucks should be able to make a latte.
