title: TLDR Founders 2025-10-24
text: TLDR Founders 2025-10-24 OpenAI acquisition 🤝, how to build AI products 🤖, early stage startup compensation 💰 Still briefing designers? Lovart already shipped it. (Sponsor) Lovart is the AI design agent built for founders . It learns your style, tone, and past projects so every output looks on-brand from Day 1. Now with Fast Mode, generating results up to 80% faster - because founders can't wait. By orchestrating models like Sora 2, Veo 3.1, Nano Banana and GPT-Image, it goes beyond single-model limits - creating visuals, videos, and ads in one seamless workspace. Founders are using Lovart to : 🧑‍💻 Turn simple prompts into brand-ready visuals ⚡️ Skip expensive design outsourcing 🫡 Collaborate with an AI teammate that understands context and tone Try Lovart before your co-founder does 👀 📈 Headlines & Trends David Tisch's BoxGroup raises $550 million across two new funds as venture firm crosses 16 years (3 minute read) BoxGroup, led by David Tisch, raised $550 million across two new funds, focusing on early-stage and follow-on investments. The firm adopts a collaborative approach, participating in numerous early-stage rounds alongside other VCs, building an impressive portfolio including Ramp and Stripe. Tisch emphasizes flexibility in sector and geography, aiming to maintain relevance despite growing competition for LP funding. Why AI Cannot Simulate Your Customers (4 minute read) A new study claims AI can mimic customer purchase intent with 90 percent accuracy. Impressive on paper, but founders should be cautious. Models can mirror how people talk about buying, not how they actually buy. Real decisions happen inside context: risk, politics, switching costs, and emotion. AI can help generate hypotheses, but strategy still starts in messy, human conversations. You don't find differentiators in data, you find them in people. OpenAI acquires Software Applications Incorporated, maker of Sky (2 minute read) OpenAI acquired Software Applications Incorporated, the creator of Sky, a natural language interface for macOS. This acquisition aims to integrate Sky's capabilities into ChatGPT, enhancing AI functionality across user applications. The entire Software Applications team will join OpenAI to advance this integration. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to Build AI Products That Are Actually Good (5 minute read) Making AI work in production takes more than clever prompts. When thousands of users interact with a model, every mistake – wrong tone, bad data, off response – multiplies fast. That's why companies now rely on “evals,” systems that test and score models the same way engineers test code. Tools like Braintrust let teams log real user inputs, compare outputs against expected answers, and continuously tune models based on results. It's how Notion, Ramp, and Stripe keep AI features reliable as they scale. Product-Market Fit is No Longer Static (2 minute read) AI's rapid advancements have made product-market fit (PMF) a continually shifting target. Companies must frequently re-establish PMF as evolving capabilities demand real-time adjustments and feedback. This dynamic environment fuels the need for more engineers directly engaging with customers. How Fluently Hit $5M ARR with $0 Ad Spend (3 minute read) Fluently scaled from $48K to $5M ARR in a year by treating short-form video like a data experiment, not a creative gamble. In 200 days, it pulled over a billion views across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube Shorts, and Facebook Reels without buying ads. It posted 2–3 videos daily through dozens of small creators, studied which hooks worked, then remade top performers fast with new faces. Every video was tracked with UTM links from view to revenue, cutting what didn't convert. Consistency, fast iteration, and measurement replaced big budgets and built one of the fastest UGC growth engines in SaaS. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Nimo (Tool) Unify all AI workflows into one infinite canvas. NativeMind (Tool) NativeMind is a fully private, open-source AI assistant that runs the latest models locally in your browser. Hello World Media (Tool) End to end production studio for cinematic launch videos. 🎁 Miscellaneous Eve Raises $103 Million at $1 Billion Valuation to Help Plaintiff Firms Deliver Justice Through AI Transformation (4 minute read) Eve raised $103 million in Series B funding, led by Spark Capital, reaching a $1 billion valuation. The AI platform now serves over 450 law firms, processing 200,000 legal cases annually and assisting in recovering over $3.5 billion in settlements. Eve's rapidly growing legal AI platform aims to reimagine plaintiff firms' operations by enhancing efficiency and scaling case capacity. What Early Stage Founders Should Know About Comp (5 minute read) Early-stage founders don't need Google-style compensation systems. What you need is something simple enough to explain in five minutes and fair enough that no one questions it. Start by defining your philosophy: how much cash versus equity, how transparent you'll be, and what principles guide every decision. This foundation matters because you'll use it to justify every offer you make. From there, the math is straightforward. Pay people fairly but not lavishly, since the real upside is equity. Keep your first ten hires under 10% of the total pool so you have room to grow, then calibrate by role: sales earns on revenue, customer success on retention, product on outcomes. When someone proves their impact, reward them immediately. How Palo Alto + CyberArk could reshape identity security (6 minute read) Palo Alto's $25B deal for CyberArk is more than another big cybersecurity acquisition—it's a signal that identity is becoming the center of enterprise defense. CyberArk has long been the go-to for protecting the most powerful accounts inside companies, while Palo Alto built its reputation securing networks and clouds. Together, they can turn identity into the main line of defense, deciding who gets access, when, and under what conditions. The hard part will be keeping CyberArk's trusted focus intact as Palo Alto folds it into a much larger platform. ⚡️ Quick Links Good Products, Built to Last (1 minute read) Founders obsess over faster, smarter, newer, but somewhere along the way, we stopped building things people actually love to use. Who Will Buy The SaaS Companies? (1 minute read) For years, SaaS founders knew the playbook: reach $20–50M ARR, keep growing, and buyers would line up. In Sync: The Hidden Rhythms That Shape Us (1 minute read) We mirror those around us more than we think. text: TLDR Founders 2025-10-27 AWS outage 🔴, Snap AR glasses 👓, Crusoe raises $1.38B 💰 A handful of TLDR founders will get $1,000 off a Wing Assistant (Sponsor) Ever wish you could offload 70% of your busywork? This exclusive offer is for you. A select number of TLDR founders can unlock $1,000 off their first Wing Executive Assistant plan. Wing only hires the top 0.1% of half a million annual applicants , allowing founders to reclaim 30–80h/week for higher-value work. 👉 Marketing, sales, admin, software- Wing has assistants for 22+ service lines, currently serving 10K+ clients with highest average client tenure in the industry. Whether you're pitching your first investors, scaling through Series A, or leading a global enterprise, Wing has an assistant for you. Claim your credit before it's gone 📈 Headlines & Trends Amazon's AWS nears recovery after major outage disrupts apps, services worldwide (7 minute read) An AWS outage in its northern Virginia data center, US-EAST-1, disrupted major sites like Snapchat and Reddit globally. The issue, rooted in DNS problems with the DynamoDB API, impacted platforms in gaming, finance, and more. AWS is addressing the problem, but lingering issues persist. AI data center startup Crusoe raising $1.38 billion in latest funding round (1 minute read) Crusoe is raising $1.38 billion in a Series E round led by Valor Equity Partners and Mubadala Capital, valuing the company at $10 billion. Key investors include Nvidia, Fidelity Management, and Founders Fund. Crusoe recently launched the first phase of its 1.2 gigawatt data center in Abilene, Texas. Snap eyes $1 billion fundraise for AR glasses (1 minute read) Snap aims to raise $1 billion for its AR glasses division. CEO Evan Spiegel is negotiating with potential investors, including Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund. This fundraising effort highlights Snap's commitment to advancing its AR technology. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Why Pitches Fail (4 minute read) Most startup pitches miss because founders try to explain too much. They know every corner of the business and assume investors need to, too. In ten minutes, that turns into a firehose of slides, acronyms, and backstory. However, a pitch is less about teaching and more about sparking interest. Investors don't need to understand everything, they just need to care enough to ask for another meeting. The best decks keep it simple: one clear problem, one sharp solution, a few proof points, and a story that makes sense. How This Startup Found Product-Market Fit in Fintech (5 minute read) Casca shows how product-market fit happens when startups move from guessing to learning directly from customers. The team embedded inside banks, observing how lenders worked and solving problems as they appeared, instead of relying on assumptions or slide decks. That focus turned slow, manual loan approvals into an automated process that banks now depend on. The lesson is clear: PMF is rarely a perfect product or vision. It comes from sitting close enough to customers to feel their pain, building something that fixes it, and repeating the cycle until demand pulls you forward. Turning First Meetings Into Real Pipeline (4 minute read) If first calls aren't turning into deals, don't just push for more meetings – figure out why. Start by collecting data: run call transcripts through an LLM or have reps record structured feedback. Are you talking to the right companies, the right buyers, and saying the right things? Then tighten stage definitions so “qualified” means the same thing to everyone. Use a scorecard to rate each call on discovery, pain, and next steps to see where reps fall short. From there, build training around the patterns, role-play for storytelling, ICP refinement for targeting, and coach on urgency when deals stall. Fix the system before you add volume. Clear criteria and consistent coaching turns activity into actual pipeline. ⚒️ Tools & Resources a16z on who's winning in the AI monetization race (Sponsor) AI is transforming products and redefining how software companies make money. Hear from Martin Casado, General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz, in this live webinar on how leading startups and public companies are building new monetization models for the AI era. Register for the webinar . Fabi.ai (Tool) Fabi.ai lets you build, deploy, and share AI-powered data analyst agents that combine SQL, Python, and AI automation for fast, collaborative analysis. Imagine AI (Tool) Automate your B2B content pipeline so prospects find you, trust you, and book meetings before your competitors even get a reply. Dynamic Mockups (Tool) Dynamic Mockups auto-generates bulk, studio-quality product mockups for e-commerce using your own Photoshop templates via API and integrations. 🎁 Miscellaneous The Divine Lever (4 minute read) Why do well-meaning founders struggle to focus on customers? Perhaps it's philosophical. Most founders operate from self: our goals, our vision, our version of progress. This author's answer is the divine lever: a pursuit that is purely good, rooted entirely in serving demand. When you find that lever, effort feels effortless because it pulls you instead of draining you. A Very Obvious Deal (6 minute read) Software pricing runs on psychology as much as math. Slack's promise to charge only for active users made people expect fairness tied to usage, and that mindset spread across SaaS. Once buyers learned to ask what something costs to run instead of what it's worth to them, value became harder to defend. That's dbt's problem: it powers data teams but doesn't handle the “expensive” part like storage or compute, so it looks easy to underprice. Fivetran brings scale and alignment, but not a fix to that perception. The merger matters less as a product move and more as a lesson in how pricing shapes who captures value in the modern data stack. Power Tools (3 minute read) Every craft has a tool powerful enough to both create and destroy. In woodworking, it's the table saw. In software, it's the IDE. Mastering them takes patience, planning, and respect. The same rule applies to design and AI tools today: no-code and generators let anyone build fast, but speed without understanding leads to fragile results. They're great for sketches, not for structures meant to last. Knowing when to reach for power and when to work by hand is what separates craft from chaos. ⚡️ Quick Links The Math of Hypergrowth: Two Paths to the Same Goal (2 minute read) Slack and Atlassian both reached significant market caps with differing timelines to $1 billion revenue—Slack in 7 years, Atlassian in 17. Alter Egos and Getting Off Social Media (1 minute read) This founder writes about realizing how much of his drive came from ego disguised as purpose. Verticalized Voice AI – The Next Application Layer Shift (3 minute read) Voice AI is poised to revolutionize software interfaces by integrating voice as the primary interaction layer, transforming workflows in regulated industries like healthcare, finance, and logistics. Your first million MAUs are free. Fireworks focuses on customizable AI with improved inference speed and cost, helping companies use specific data for tailored AI solutions. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Assessing Overemphasized Startup Metrics (4 minute read) Startups often inflate sales and partnership metrics, so investors should check documentation for accuracy. Historical performance and meaningful metrics, such as CAC and LTV, reveal execution capability and business model efficiency. Patents are not enough in tech-driven startups. Assess commercialization and pilot program outcomes to gauge viability. A Research Thinking Toolkit to Speed Up the Discovery Phase (26 minute read) Jeanette Mellinger urges founders to prioritize problem-solution fit over speed, using a research toolkit adapted from UX practices. This approach helps founders understand customer needs, behaviors, and workflows, ensuring a product-market fit and a resilient team foundation. Mellinger outlines three phases of research (Incubate, Immerse, and Integrate), providing methods to master each element of founder, problem, and solution fit, leading to sustainable and effective growth strategies. Mercury's Path to Product-Market Fit (5 minute read) Mercury's story shows that product-market fit doesn't come from iteration speed but from doing the hardest thing first. Before writing a line of code, founder Immad Akhund spent months talking to founders, lawyers, and investors to understand how fintech actually worked and where it broke. That homework revealed the real barrier wasn't product design but regulation and trust. So the team built slowly and deliberately: a fully digital business account that could replace a traditional bank from day one, not a lightweight fintech add-on. They rebuilt the product twice, swapped out an underperforming partner bank, and refused to launch until it worked end to end. When Mercury finally went live, 1,500 companies signed up in the first week, one depositing $1 million without ever speaking to the team. ⚒️ Tools & Resources 🪽 Offload 70% of Your Busywork to an experienced Wing Assistant (Sponsor) It's hard to prioritize urgent tasks when small tasks stack up. Wing Assistants blend human precision and AI speed to take every unwanted chore off your plate: admin, operations, social media, outreach emails, and more. Hire your first Wing Assistant with $1,000 off your first four months - including setup, training, and hand off. The Hog (Tool) The “Google Maps” for customer acquisition. Folderly AI (Tool) Folderly AI generates high-performing, spam-checked cold email sequences with AI, tailored to your offer, audience, and goals. Portia AI (Tool) Portia AI lets developers build safe, compliant AI agents with human-readable plans, execution tracking, and guardrails for regulated industries. 🎁 Miscellaneous Thoughts on the AI Buildout (4 minute read) AI growth is facing physical limits. Nvidia's profits could fund several new TSMC fabs, which means chip demand now exceeds the cost of producing them. The real constraints are power, materials, and labor. The world needs more factories for turbines and transformers, and hundreds of thousands of skilled workers to wire and cool datacenters. Speed now matters more than efficiency, so natural gas is beating nuclear and solar for new power. Hyperscalers are moving off-grid, building modular datacenters, and paying suppliers more to accelerate production. If growth continues, tens of gigawatts of new capacity will come online each year. Even if the boom slows, it will leave behind a massive industrial base ready to deliver cheap, rapid compute for decades. Data & AI Infrastructure Are Fusing (2 minute read) AI innovation requires a new data architecture focused on real-time feedback, speed, and accuracy. Companies like Netflix and Stripe pivot to unified systems with vector and context databases for rapid model updates. This necessitates integrated observability layers to provide comprehensive insight into data and AI health. The Future of the Web Is the History of YouTube (5 minute read) Fifteen years ago, people making YouTube videos looked like hobbyists in an already crowded world of content. In hindsight, the world was actually short on video, waiting for tools that made creation easy. Software is at that same point now. Large language models let anyone with an idea build an app without writing code, turning small, niche ideas into real products. This shift opens the long tail of software that was never worth hiring a team to build. The people driving it will look less like engineers and more like creators who understand audiences and storytelling. The web is becoming more participatory again, powered by people who make things because they can, not because they have permission. ⚡️ Quick Links When ARR is not ARR (1 minute read) ARR was meant to represent predictable annual subscription revenue, but many startups now stretch it into fiction. text: TLDR Founders 2025-10-31 Grammarly rebrands to Superhuman 🦸, Meta’s AI push 🤖, $555B of cloud spend 💰 a16z on How AI is Rewriting the Rules of SaaS Monetization (Sponsor) In Metronome's upcoming webinar , Martin Casado, General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz, reveals how leading startups and public companies alike are building monetization operating models for the AI era. You'll learn: ⏩ Why AI is accelerating the shift from traditional subscriptions to results-based and hybrid monetization models 💵 How fast-growing AI companies are building their Monetization Operating Models to align product, finance, GTM, and engineering 🤔 Why treating pricing like a product enables experimentation and growth Save your seat 📈 Headlines & Trends $555B of Cloud Spend (1 minute read) Microsoft and Google's latest earnings reveal the sheer scale of the AI boom. Together, they're sitting on $555 billion in cloud commitments. Both companies are doubling down on data centers, pushing toward a $100B annualized CapEx each. Microsoft plans to expand AI capacity by 80% and double its data center footprint within two years, while Google raised its 2025 CapEx forecast to $93B. Microsoft's commercial backlog has surged past $400B, and Google Cloud's hit $155B, signaling long-term demand strong enough to justify spending more than their entire cash reserves. Meta's AI Push Works, But Wall Street Isn't Convinced (2 minute read) Meta's stock dropped 8% after earnings, even though its AI improvements are clearly working. People spend 10% more time on Instagram because of better AI recommendations, and advertisers using Meta's AI tools pay 14% less per lead. The company now makes $60 billion from AI-powered ads. The problem is expenses - they jumped 32% as Zuckerberg pours money into AI research labs faster than revenue is growing. Meta grew revenue 26% and engagement is up across every app, but investors think it is spending too aggressively on AI when the current tools already deliver results. Grammarly rebrands to 'Superhuman,' launches a new AI assistant (2 minute read) Grammarly has rebranded to "Superhuman" after acquiring the email client Superhuman and launching an AI assistant called Superhuman Go. The AI assistant, integrated with Grammarly's extension, connects with apps like Jira and Google Calendar, enhancing its functionality for tasks like scheduling and data fetching. With its transition to a productivity suite, Grammarly aims to compete with Notion and Google Workspace by offering enhanced AI features. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to Find Your Wedge (4 minute read) Startups die trying to build everything at once. Founders hear “go after a big market” and start designing the Maximum Viable Product, a sprawling vision that burns time, cash, and morale before it ever meets a customer. The smarter move is finding your wedge: one tightly scoped problem your small team can solve better than anyone else. Amazon started with books, and Facebook started with college photos. Each nailed a small problem and earned the right to expand. How to Keep Winning (3 minute read) Replit's founder says winning is less about luck and more about surviving long enough to compound. The first rule is simple: don't die. Know your red lines, manage downside, and stay in the game even when things look bleak. The second is never quit, progress compounds slowly until it suddenly doesn't. Easy paths rarely lead to lasting advantage, so lean into hard problems that others avoid. Winning requires focus: lock in, study competitors, and learn from their mistakes. While shortcuts are tempting, playing by the rules forces innovation that becomes a moat. Finally, give back. Sharing ideas, open-sourcing work, and helping others doesn't weaken you, it builds goodwill and compounds success over time. When to Hire Your First PM (5 minute read) Every founder hits a moment when a product stops being the best use of their time. The roadmap fills with new features, customer feedback piles up, and you're juggling sprint planning while trying to close deals and hire your next engineer. That's when it's time to bring in your first PM. Before PMF, you should own product yourself, but once the feedback loops multiply and tactical decisions start eating into growth, you need someone who can synthesize, prioritize, and keep momentum. ⚒️ Tools & Resources See how Ragie removes the RAG busywork (Sponsor) Learn how teams ship AI features faster with a fully managed RAG pipeline. Ragie's agentic retrieval breaks down complex questions, searches across sources, self evaluates, and delivers the right context to your AI application so your team can stay focused on building what matters most. See how it works → Schedule a call → Alloy (Tool) Prototypes that look exactly like your product. Unicorn Studio (Tool) Create enchanting WebGL effects, motion, and interactivity. Skala (Tool) Skala is an all-in-one legal platform for startups to incorporate, fundraise, manage IP, hire, and access legal services globally. 🎁 Miscellaneous The Cult of Busyness (5 minute read) If exhaustion is your status symbol, you are paying prestige with your life. This essay skewers our worship of constant doing, where packed calendars masquerade as meaning and self-care turns into another productivity hack. Busyness becomes a sedative for anxiety and a costume for relevance, rewarded with applause even as it erodes attention, intimacy, and art. The cure is not a better app but permission to be idle on purpose, to treat stillness as a practice rather than a failure, and to stop confusing motion with a life. Will AI Slop Kill The Creator Economy? (6 minute read) Meta's Vibes and OpenAI's Sora 2 are AI tools that generate video content effortlessly, posing a threat to parts of the creator economy by replacing roles like fashion models and brand spokespeople. Despite this, genuine online creators that offer authenticity and unique insights remain valuable, as AI struggles to replicate these qualities. The proliferation of AI-generated content could threaten the media industry by oversaturating the market, but human-driven content retains its appeal due to its originality. Pivots, Paths, and Prototypes (3 minute read) You know it is time to pivot when perfecting the draft takes longer than saying what is true. A TEDx prep that would not click becomes the story of switching themes with a month to spare, proving that quitting one path can be the bravest way forward. The lesson is to build “beta” moments into work and life, test fits before committing, and judge effort by learning rate, not sunk cost. Some turns are not detours at all. They are the road revealing itself. ⚡️ Quick Links Replication (1 minute read) Every startup begins as a copy of something that worked before, but survival depends on what you choose not to copy. 10 Habits of Successful Employees (2 minute read) Top performers deliver on core priorities, only take on tasks they can finish, and keep commitments without fail. The Permission to Not Know (2 minute read) Every founder feels like a fraud the moment they start doing something that actually matters. text: TLDR Founders 2025-11-05 Sequoia’s new stewards 🏆, the new YC 🚀, ElevenLabs story 👋 Aligning pricing with AI-driven growth models: What leaders at OpenAI, Zapier, and Salesforce are saying (Sponsor) At Metronome's Monetize 2025 conference, one thing was clear: pricing has gone from a tactical play to a board-level conversation, as companies grapple with ways to align value with monetization in the age of AI : Zapier moved from pure PLG to enterprise sales. Temporal opened sales-led infrastructure to self-serve. Salesforce introduced "flex agreements" trading seats for consumption. Top operators are focused on building systems where PLG and SLG coexist, creating predictability in usage-based models, and establishing clear ownership via Chief Value Officer roles. Read the full breakdown on the Metronome blog 📈 Headlines & Trends On the ‘new' Y Combinator (5 minute read) YC's latest batches look like a time warp to its early days, but with an AI-era twist. The average founder is now 26, often a Stanford or MIT grad who left OpenAI, Stripe, or Google to build fast in San Francisco. Nearly 85% of YC startups are Bay Area–based, half the founders come from top 20 universities, and almost as many worked at top-tier employers. They're technical, pedigreed, and surrounded by alumni who've done it before. Sequoia Capital transitions to new leadership (2 minute read) Sequoia Capital has elevated Alfred Lin and Pat Grady to firm stewards, succeeding Roelof Botha. Lin and Grady led Sequoia's early and growth-stage investments, backing companies like Airbnb, DoorDash, HubSpot, and Zoom. Botha, known for creating the scout program and leading fund restructuring, will remain with the firm. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics When the Music's Over: Founder Life After Failure (6 minute read) Founders should set clear financial boundaries, track personal runway, and avoid personal debt while running a startup. Shifting quickly into advisory or consulting roles helps monetize lessons learned and regain momentum. Openly sharing failures builds credibility and signals readiness for future opportunities. Less-Is-Better Effect (3 minute read) A $16 NARS mini concealer costs more than double per ounce than the full size, yet it outsells it. Psychologist Christopher Hsee found that when we judge one thing in isolation, our brains anchor on what's easy to notice, not what's objectively better. A small cup overflowing with ice cream feels richer than a big one half empty. The same logic drives how we buy. A tiny luxury feels premium, a pre-portioned meal kit feels smart, a mini beauty product feels indulgent. Where you build is who you are: the ElevenLabs story (6 minute read) ElevenLabs didn't start in San Francisco, and that might be its biggest advantage. Born from the frustration of single-voice dubs in Poland, the company built an AI that gives machines human nuance - tone, emotion, and accent - shaped by a team spread across London, Warsaw, and San Francisco. Geography became the edge: only people who've heard meaning flattened by translation would obsess over making voice universal again. The company's rulebook is pragmatic: if research can't solve a problem in three months, building a good product will. Work with creatives instead of against them. Pay artists for their voices. Scale globally, but keep the culture weird and local. ⚒️ Tools & Resources 🆕 Humanity Protocol integrates Mastercard's Open Finance into Human ID (Sponsor) Verifying personal financial information can often be time-consuming and involve manual touchpoints. Humanity Protocol's Human ID now integrates Mastercard's open finance connectivity, enabling users to verify personal financial information such as income level, cash flow, and asset ownership. Users can use reusable and privacy-preserving credentials across traditional and decentralized financial platforms. Learn more. String.com (Tool) String.com is an AI agent that helps you build, run, and deploy other AI agents in seconds. LLM Gateway (Tool) LLM Gateway lets you route, manage, and analyze AI model requests from multiple providers through a single unified API. a0.dev (Tool) a0.dev is an AI platform that instantly generates full React Native apps, complete with UI, backend, payments, and App Store deployment. 🎁 Miscellaneous What if we are the bubble? (4 minute read) Everyone keeps wondering if AI is the bubble. What if the bubble is us? For decades, we've treated human labor (especially knowledge work) as sacred and scarce, when much of it is just bureaucracy in disguise. Startups bragged about headcount, not output. Corporations paid armies to copy, check, and forward information. Now AI is quietly marking those tasks to market. The Only Thing That Matters (6 minute read) In business, everything feels urgent until you notice that only one thing actually changes the outcome. Jim Kilts saw that at Gillette. Faced with seven turnaround options and endless noise, he picked one core issue in each line, kept Duracell and fixed marketing discipline, pulled personal care out from razors, focused only on dry shaving, and ignored the rest. Ask what truly drives the result, drop the rest fast, and repeat until it becomes instinct. That's how good operators think and how wisdom begins. A simple test for developing taste (4 minute read) Taste isn't about what you like—it's about how your preferences change once you've seen something better. You read Middlemarch after Remains of the Day, and suddenly the scale resets. You can't unsee the difference. That's how taste develops: through comparison, awareness, and the slow upgrading of what you think good means. It happens in every field - music, design, writing, code. The more you expose yourself to better work, the clearer your sense of what fits. ⚡️ Quick Links Circular Financing: Does Nvidia's $110B Bet Echo the Telecom Bubble? (7 minute read) Nvidia's $110 billion vendor financing strategy in AI, notably through its commitment to OpenAI, draws comparisons to the telecom bubble's risky loans. AI as the greatest source of empowerment for all (10 minute read) AI democratizes knowledge by offering personalized insights, doubling learning rates compared to traditional methods. AI Wins When It Does the GTM Work People Won't (2 minute read) An AI SDR booked a six-figure meeting while the team was at dinner at 6:02 PM on a Saturday - no human rep would have done that, and that's the point. title: TLDR Founders 2025-11-07
text: TLDR Founders 2025-11-07 VC is not competitive 👊, OpenAI’s revenue 💰, how You.com survived ChatGPT 🔍 How Startups Can Use CX to Drive Break-out Growth (Sponsor) Dear Founder, Scaling your startup isn't just about building a great product—it's about building a great experience. The fastest-growing startups treat customer experience as a growth engine, not a support function. Ubiquity shares how early CX investment drives loyalty, retention, and long-term valuation . In this blog , you'll learn how to: Build CX foundations early to turn customers into advocates. Use data and feedback loops to sharpen product-market fit. Design scalable support that grows with your customer base. See how top founders are leveraging CX to accelerate sustainable growth. Read more. 📈 Headlines & Trends Venture Capital is Not Competitive (4 minute read) Venture capital no longer behaves like a competitive market. The language of rivalry persists, but the substance has thinned. The same firms crowd around the same deals, rehearsing the same convictions until they harden into consensus. Somewhere along the way, the infinite game became finite. What began as a search for outliers has become an exercise in pattern recognition. Capital pools, performance converges, and the illusion of competition masks a quiet retreat from risk. The question is not who wins this round, but whether there's still a game being played at all. The $100B Question: How SaaS Giants Are Rewriting the Rules of Value with AI in 2025 (10 minute read) SaaS pricing ran on predictability for two decades. Seats and flat plans made sense when value scaled with people. AI broke that logic - one agent can do the work of ten, and each query can carry a volatile compute bill. Companies are now rebuilding their models in real time. Hybrid pricing buys time but not clarity. Outcome pricing promises fairness but demands proof. Between the two lies a growing realization that software was predictable only because the people using it were. Sam Altman on OpenAI's Revenue (1 minute read) OpenAI's annual revenue run rate has surpassed $20B. Future infrastructure commitments are reaching $1.4 trillion. The company's plans include enterprise offerings, AI cloud services, and ventures into consumer devices and scientific discovery. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Look For Yes (2 minute read) Product and legal teams often clash due to misunderstood risk levels. Risk managers should ensure their advice isn't seen as an absolute veto but rather as guidance by framing context and trade-offs. Product managers need to actively seek solutions to legal concerns, aiming for responsible risk-taking rather than defaulting to "no." How You.com Survived ChatGPT With a Pivot to Enterprise AI (8 minute read) You.com shifted from consumer search to enterprise AI, offering search APIs and complex data analytics to businesses following ChatGPT's launch. Competing with Google and others in the AI market, You.com leverages its own search infrastructure to deliver detailed reports and agent-based solutions like business analytics and financial analysis. The company also introduced Auto Mode for integrating multiple LLMs and secured $100M in VC funding to expand its agent-centric services. Why OpenAI wants a piece of AMD (8 minute read) OpenAI will buy up to 6 GW of AMD GPUs, starting with 1 GW of MI450 in H2 2026, and get $0.01 warrants for up to 160M shares that vest with 1–6 GW deployments and other milestones, potentially reaching ~10% ownership. It chose AMD because Nvidia supply is tight and pricey, AMD claims MI450 will beat current Nvidia parts, and AMD's smaller base trades equity for multi‑GW orders. Monetization trails GPU demand, especially in video, and tariff‑driven inflation cuts rate‑cut odds, raising downside risk for AI‑levered names. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Breaking: 🥊 The Great Startup Debate: 996 vs. The 4-Day Work Week (Sponsor) Some founders swear by 12-hour days and 6-day weeks. Others claim the 4-day work week builds better teams and products. Who's right? On Nov 19, join Varun Rana as two early-stage founders face off in a live debate with Q&A, audience polls, and free prizes. It's fast, funny, and brutally honest…the future of work, unfiltered. 👉 Register now to watch it live. Venture Codex (Tool) An investor database for startups looking to raise. Sigma Mind (Tool) Build human-like conversations with AI Agnes AI (Tool) Agnes AI is a collaborative AI agent that lets teams co-edit, share memory, and build slides, reports, and strategy docs together in real time. 🎁 Miscellaneous Koah Launches: AdSense for GenAI (3 minute read) Koah introduces AdSense-like monetization for GenAI apps by integrating context-aware ads into chat. With proven success in apps like Liner and Luzia, developers see high revenue without compromising user experience, averaging $10 ECPM and 3-5x higher engagement rates. The Lost Art of Crying (5 minute read) Former Facebook VP Julie Zhuo just wrote the most vulnerable thing you'll read about founder psychology this year. She reveals how Silicon Valley's "ice queen" culture (the obsession with logic, optimization, and emotional control) is actually a fear response, not a superpower. As AI takes over rational work like calculations and planning, the only irreplaceable human skill is our ability to truly feel: grief, awe, connection, vulnerability. Zhuo discovered this through what she calls "Big Cries" using music and solitude, and argues that in an era where we're building superhuman machines, learning to cry again isn't weakness but reclaiming the last thing AI can never touch. The Internet Wants to Be Fragmented (5 minute read) The social media empire is crumbling, and Gen Z is leading the exodus. Nearly half of Gen Z plans to spend less time on social networks in 2025, abandoning the "town square" platforms for intimate Discord channels, DMs, and curated feeds. The big experiment of throwing humanity into one digital room failed. This shift back to small, self-selected communities is a massive opportunity for builders creating tools for micro-communities instead of trying to be the next Twitter. ⚡️ Quick Links How to Get Feedback (3 minute read) When was the last time someone told you the truth about your work? This strategy, driven by intense talent competition and regulatory uncertainty, helps companies expedite AI development and maintain a competitive edge. The company emphasizes AI-driven productivity in fields like healthcare, banking, and retail, alongside major investments in cloud infrastructure, security, and quantum computing. Key efforts include launching 400+ AI-enabled datacenters, expanding AI Copilots with 100 million monthly active users, and committing $4 billion to AI education initiatives, while aiming for sustainability with increased renewable energy and water efficiency goals. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to Help Your Head of Marketing Win Early (6 minute read) When a new Head of Marketing joins, everyone rushes in with ideas - new deck, new brand, new campaign - and nothing tied to pipeline gets finished. Slow the chaos early. Align on two or three measurable goals for the first 90 days, make them public, and be explicit about what marketing isn't touching yet. Then help them deliver something visible that earns trust - refresh the homepage, fix demo follow-ups, or launch one targeted campaign that actually brings in leads. What Makes a Round Easy (4 minute read) Every round falls into one of two buckets. Either you're an attention spike - the kind of team, traction, or market that investors chase on instinct - or you're an explainer, where belief builds through conversation and time. This post breaks down how to tell which you are and why it matters. Your Roadmap is a Prototype for Your Strategy (12 minute read) Product roadmaps should be treated as prototypes, focusing on adaptability and learning, rather than rigid delivery contracts. A flexible roadmap, like the Now-Next-Later model, encourages teams to test assumptions, iterate based on feedback, and keep strategies aligned with changing environments. By emphasizing the process over the end product, roadmapping builds resilience and ensures strategies remain relevant and effective. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Reach millions of tech professionals at scale (Sponsor) Over 6 million tech professionals read TLDR including developers, product managers, marketers, designers and executives. Some are missionaries who believe deeply in a mission, others are mercenaries who love the chase. There are performers too - the rebels who bend the rules and the ones who fake it until it breaks. People move between these types as they grow, fail, or win. What matters most isn't which one you start as, but who you're becoming while you build. ⚡️ Quick Links “The API is the Product” is a Lie (3 minute read) Everyone loves the “API-as-a-product” idea. Provision customers instantly without engineering involvement. 🪜 Account hierarchy: Create parent–child contract structures. Roll up usage, commitments, and reporting into a single organization. Read more on the Metronome blog 📈 Headlines & Trends The First $100,000,000 ARR at Datadog (5 minute read) It's hard to imagine what real customer focus looks like before a company has revenue, but Datadog built it in from the start. The founders spent six months interviewing users before writing code, opened betas so customers could self-select, and sold month to month to spot churn immediately. That approach scaled to 700 employees and $100 million ARR. Engineers still rotate through support, pricing talks double as product feedback, and leadership treats every complaint as a signal. The result is a culture designed to find bad news fast and fix it before it compounds. Founders And Investors Slam UK “Exit Tax” (1 minute read) The UK government is considering a 20% “exit tax” on founders who build businesses in Britain but move abroad to avoid taxes. More than 150 entrepreneurs and investors - behind companies like Beamery, Cleo, and funds including Dawn Capital and 20VC - have pushed back, warning it would punish success and drive talent out. They say Britain already feels less competitive after capital gains hikes, and another tax like this could be the final push for many to leave. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to actually raise your round (24 minute read) Genomines' founder spent 14 months doing exactly the same thing: he pitched the same company over hundreds of conversations, facing the same objections and polite rejections. Then he closed $45 million from 12 funds. This post looks at what it really takes to raise a round. It's a messy and non-linear process. Startup Operating Principles (2 minute read) Sam Gerstenzang grew from a fine to a good operator in three and a half years of working at Stripe. This post shares how Stripe approaches decision-making and communication. Operating well is a state. Peak performance is a constant process of tweaking, iterating, and tuning. Strategism: Mastering the Founder Letter (7 minute read) In an era of formulaic marketing and AI-written content, the founder letter stands out as one of the few tools that still feels human. It works because it earns trust through story instead of shouting for attention - naming a problem, stirring emotion, and only then offering the solution. From Jason Fried's product launches to the plainspoken clarity of Jeff Bezos and Warren Buffett, the format proves that narrative still builds belief and conviction. Even small tests show it moves results: pages using a founder's voice convert higher because readers can tell when something was written by a person who actually means it. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Exclusive offer: Hire and pay international contractors FREE until 2027 (Sponsor) Justworks has an exclusive offer for growing businesses like yours: access top talent across the globe - without the hidden fees. And you get all the benefits of Justworks: support for 60+ countries and currencies, and access to add-on features like time tracking, health insurance, or full-time hiring abroad. The company is a vehicle to accomplish a goal that would otherwise be impossible alone. Many founders have found wealth, yet they reinvest it to reach further. Many people work for the fruit - it is rare to find people who work for the work itself. The Compounding Startup (6 minute read) An analysis of 6,525 software companies revealed that outliers and "normies" had nearly identical metrics at $1M ARR (the top 25% of failures actually grew faster than half the eventual winners). The difference emerged in relentless operational improvement: 86% of outliers increased expansion revenue by 10%+, 72% boosted ARPA significantly, and 51% improved retention - all while “normies” stayed mostly flat. The compound effect is staggering as a 50% improvement in pricing for new customers doubled annual growth rates from 11% to 24%, while combining better pricing and retention took growth "off the charts." Software Is The New Art Form (3 minute read) Just as furniture, fashion, and food progressed from essentialist basics to art forms that make you feel something, software is speedrunning the same transformation. The frontier is now defined by software that either expands what's possible or makes you feel - and occasionally does both. ⚡️ Quick Links Optimizing The Landing Page (1 minute read) Tweaking and obsessing over your conversion rates won't save you if the wrong people are showing up. This piece breaks down personality message fit, a second kind of PMF where you stop mimicking “leader voice” and build around how you naturally think, talk, and react. It shows how to audit your baseline, prep with 30-second micro-clarity, read implicit feedback, sharpen your spikes, and tweak delivery like an experiment. Communication becomes a system you design, not a performance you fake. Engineers will have large usage fluctuations, so startups need to price accordingly to insulate against that. The typical way of doing this is to charge users a subscription fee and bill usage on top of that. Anyone who's opened a Miro board knows this truth. People create beautifully messy diagrams with overlapping lenses because one view is never enough to capture reality. This article breaks down nine different lenses teams use simultaneously: customer journey flows, persona mapping, Wardley Map axes showing capability evolution and user proximity, team boundaries and interactions, opportunities and options, and execution workflows. BlackBerry listened to feedback about wanting better keyboards and perfected them, yet completely ignored the shift toward touchscreens that the iPhone represented. History shows it failed spectacularly. The difference comes down to the Einstellung effect, where expertise creates blindness to better solutions. Founders need to distinguish temporary customer truths like physical keyboards from permanent human needs like efficient communication. This article offers frameworks, including physics-first analysis and monthly anti-expertise audits, where you argue against your strongest beliefs. ⚡️ Quick Links A Good Business Vs A Useful Idea (1 minute read) A “good idea” is something useful or clever, but it only becomes a business when you turn it into something people can't easily substitute and will actually pay a premium for. Metrics often need time to show value, and sharing ownership across teams can drive better outcomes. Platform innovations, though less visible, play crucial roles in long-term success by enhancing scalability, efficiency, and competitive differentiation. The unit economics alone make it almost impossible to justify hiring humans for many functions. Tutoring an AI is much easier in many cases. However, an AI team is a quiet one - it can be lonely when group meetings are gone and there's no one to grab after work drinks with. I analyzed 1,000 forward deployed engineer jobs: what I learned… (18 minute read) Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) are software engineers who embed directly with customers to build, deploy, and maintain complex technical systems in production. They are like a hybrid of a senior software engineer and a technical consultant. The role is exploding in growth because AI deployment is hard - companies need engineers who can ensure complex AI systems actually work in the real world. title: TLDR Founders 2025-11-24
text: TLDR Founders 2025-11-24 AI rewiring economy 📊, the PMF treadmill 🏃, odds of hitting $1M ARR 💰 How Lovable solves AI monetization: A conversation with Elena Verna (Sponsor) As AI reshapes how software delivers value, traditional SaaS pricing models are starting to break down. In this live webinar , Elena Verna, Lovable's Head of Growth, will share the monetization model the team is building to navigate this shift. Join to learn: Why AI has upended the logic of SaaS pricing, and why there's still no clear playbook How Lovable is balancing self-serve adoption with enterprise monetization What it takes to evolve from usage-based pricing to outcome-based value See where AI monetization is heading and what you can do to prepare your organization today. Join the conversation live on December 10 📈 Headlines & Trends AI is Rewiring the Economy (16 minute read) AI will force us to reconsider commerce, consumerism, and the norms of our economy. It will commoditize knowledge work, destroying consumer purchasing power. Human behavior will shift from consumption to goal-seeking as AI handles routine tasks. New business models will emerge that profit from human flourishing rather than human spending. Consumerism will cease to be the driver of economic growth. The Product-Market Fit Treadmill: Why every AI company is sprinting just to stay in place (7 minute read) Product-Market Fit (PMF) used to be something companies would hit, maintain, and scale. The core value propositions for AI companies are up for grabs every week, causing them to flat-out sprint but still stay in the same place. PMF is now a subscription that companies have to keep renewing. Stable PMF will become possible again when we move into the value cycle. This will be when real enterprise value is created. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics The odds of making it (10 minute read) Reaching $25 million ARR within 10 years is harder than getting into Harvard. Only 1 in 50 startups achieves this milestone. Almost half of software startups make it to $1 million ARR, though it usually takes 2-5 years. AI-native startups are 3X more likely to hit $1 million ARR in six months compared to others. While living in the Bay Area helps, it's not essential for success. RB2B's AI Playbook: 95% of Customer Interaction Handled by AI (4 minute read) Founders need to treat AI like their best employee - train it daily, give it the resources it needs, and hold it accountable for results. AI is only as good as the content it is fed. Detailed instructions give AI the ability to handle tough queries. Founders need to do the actual work if they want to replace entire teams with AI. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Ambient Daily Briefing: the 7 am email that preps every meeting on your calendar (Sponsor) Founders lose time prepping for important meetings; customers, investors, etc. With Ambient you get a one-page, presidential-style briefing every morning that's based on your calendar, sourced by an AI agent. No noise, just signal, so you can crush the day. Free 7-day trial, 30 second setup. Clever (Tool) Clever is an AI explainer that uses voice and live visuals to break down complex topics like a hyper-animated tutor on a digital whiteboard. pg_ai_query — AI-powered SQL generation & query analysis for PostgreSQL (1 minute read) pg_ai_query is a PostgreSQL extension that brings AI-powered query development directly into Postgres. It allows users to generate SQL from natural language, analyze query performance using AI interpreted EXPLAIN ANALYZE, receive index and rewrite recommendations, and leverage schema-aware query intelligence with secure introspection. pg_ai_query is designed to help developers write and tune SQL faster without switching tools to accelerate iteration across complex workloads. It integrates directly into PostgreSQL databases, providing a seamless way to query data using plain English. Mitte (Tool) Mitte is an AI creative suite for designing, animating, and voicing content with studio-grade precision and performance. 🎁 Miscellaneous Boom, bubble, bust, boom. Why should AI be different? (19 minute read) The AI revolution will only be three years old at the end of this month. We are likely months, not years, from the end of the party. While AI could be one of the biggest technology shifts in history, it will take way longer to see changes than currently predicted. The size of the current bubble is redefining how we look at previous bubbles. There is currently too much spending with too much leverage, and the deals are getting crazy. Competition with China is fierce - it is very possible that China could win the AI race. Mission, Vision, poTAYto, poTAHto (17 minute read) Purpose requires figuring out the positive difference you can make in the world, writing that succinctly, then incorporating it into your strategy, positioning, and goals. Putting words you don't mean onto an 'About Us' page doesn't create a purpose. You don't have to be a mission-driven company to succeed - most startups are too busy making stuff anyway. ⚡️ Quick Links Silly SaaS retention hack (1 minute read) Show users how well they're succeeding with your tool as soon as they log in. You Can Now Buy 'Rare' Inactive X Usernames (3 minute read) X's Handle Marketplace will allow Premium+ and Premium Business users to buy or claim usernames from old, inactive accounts. Why Netflix built fast.com (2 minute read) ISPs were slowing down Netflix on purpose, so Netflix built its own speed testing service to prove to customers that ISPs were the problem. The Minus One Mindset (4 minute read) Minus One is a way of seeing and operating that drives constant reinvention. title: TLDR Founders 2025-11-26
text: TLDR Founders 2025-11-26 Moats vs. margin 🏰, Private Equity for GTM 💰, product evals 🤖 Don't spend your early-stage funding on $180K data engineers (Sponsor) You can't run a company without data - but spending a huge chunk of your seed round on data pipeline engineers isn't sustainable. Bruin gives you an AI data engineer instead. Ask it "ingest Stripe data and calculate MRR by cohort." Bruin writes the ingestion, transformation, quality checks, and orchestration. Your report ships in minutes.. No Airbyte, no Airflow, no dbt to manage. Ask and get answers. Open-source. Managed platform. A fraction of an engineer's salary. Get enterprise data infrastructure on a seed budget 📈 Headlines & Trends Moats Before (Gross) Margins (5 minute read) Investors obsess over gross margins, but Apple has a 38% margin - roughly half of most software companies - and it's one of the most valuable businesses on earth. Margins don't determine great companies, moats - like Network effects, economies of scale, brand power, and switching costs - do. In the AI era, the obsession with high margins is even more dangerous. It might just mean your product isn't using any AI at all. Private Equity: The New Distribution Channel for AI Startups (1 minute read) Private equity firms are rapidly becoming key distribution channels for AI startups, offering access to a mid-market profile that accelerates sales cycles. AI tools that cut costs and automate workflows fit well with PE's profit motives, creating efficient deployment across portfolios. This model transforms go-to-market strategies, allowing AI startups to reach over 14,000 PE-backed companies, bypassing traditional enterprise sales approaches. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Product Evals in Three Simple Steps (9 minute read) Building evals can result in immediate payoffs. They allow you to run multiple experiments to iterate to a working product quickly. Having evals makes development much quicker than if it were bottlenecked on human annotations after each configuration change. This post details how to build product evals. Essential SaaS Metrics for a Series A Fundraise (7 minute read) Key metrics for a SaaS Series A fundraise include Annual Recurring Revenue, Monthly Recurring Revenue, and Year-over-Year growth, which demonstrate traction and scalability. Investors also focus on metrics like Customer Acquisition Cost, Lifetime Value, Net Revenue Retention, Gross Revenue Retention, and Burn Multiple to assess efficiency and growth potential. Understanding and effectively presenting these metrics can enhance credibility with investors and improve valuation. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Ambient is an AI chief of staff that preps every meeting on your calendar (Sponsor) Founder mode means being back to back all day, and still performing at your best. Ambient's AI briefing agent scours internal and external data sources to ensure you're ready for what the day brings – saving you and your team countless hours of prep work in the process. Try Ambient today, free Kite (Tool) Kite is an AI email assistant that declutters your Gmail, automates responses, and gives you back hours each week with smart, secure workflows NeetoCal (Tool) NeetoCal is an AI-powered scheduling tool that syncs calendars, handles payments, and simplifies booking for any kind of meeting. mention.click (Tool) mention is an AI-powered lead generation tool that surfaces relevant Reddit conversations for your product to enable organic outreach, validation, and customer discovery. 🎁 Miscellaneous How to get on a VC's radar in 30 days (using only LinkedIn + X) (2 minute read) You don't need followers, content, or a personal brand to get on a VC's radar - just strategic commenting. Pick 25 investors you want to meet, turn on their post notifications, and show up early with thoughtful takes. Some notes from the CEO of Spotify (2 minute read) This thread contains tips given by the CEO of Spotify during a two-hour-long presentation. Spotify made the legal option faster and easier than piracy, and it only widened its focus when its core was working. It built a reusable distribution engine and let teams own outcomes. The company invested early in experimentation infrastructure and was built for global from day one. It only bets on AI when it moves core metrics. The key to the company's success was focus, compounding iteration, and small autonomous teams with real accountability. ⚡️ Quick Links Shot called a year ago (1 minute read) Taste and judgment, not execution, are the bottlenecks when Cursor can take you from idea to code in minutes. Invert, Always Invert (3 minute read) Instead of asking how to succeed, ask how to fail, then systematically avoid those things. Stumbling (1 minute read) The smartest people in the room are often terrible communicators. title: TLDR Founders 2025-11-28
text: TLDR Founders 2025-11-28 OpenAI’s FDE playbook 📚, weak AI moats 🤖, Jony Ive interview 🎙️ Reach millions of tech professionals at scale (Sponsor) Over 6 million tech professionals read TLDR including developers, product managers, marketers, founders and executives. Get in front of your target audience with an ad placement just like this one! Learn more about running a test campaign. 📈 Headlines & Trends Our 20+ AI Agents and Their Moats: Real But Weak (11 minute read) The differentiation between AI agents is fairly narrow. While there are some differences between UX, integrations, features, and infrastructure, the core AI is basically the same everywhere. It's possible to copy from one system to another and get almost the same result. Companies need to consider what is actually defensible. Notes From an Interview With Jony Ive (3 minute read) Jony Ive emphasizes care in design, valuing attention to detail even in trivial aspects like packaging to convey thoughtfulness to users. He believes products reflect interpersonal relationships within teams, underscoring the importance of a supportive work environment. Ive also values designing in personal spaces, arguing it influences creative thinking differently than traditional conference rooms. Narrative power in the AI era (11 minute read) AI's narrative and technological advancements are tightly linked, with recent shifts from focusing on AGI to superintelligence reshaping industry goals. Companies like OpenAI and Meta are harnessing these narratives to drive capital investment and recruit top talent, illustrating how storytelling influences technological development and economic momentum. This evolving narrative reflects AI's ongoing progress, as recent breakthroughs demand a new frontier beyond human-level capabilities. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Eat Pain, Excrete Product: The FDE Playbook (4 minute read) The technology vendors that win are the ones who figure out how to make the technology actually work in the messy reality of enterprise operations. Forward Deployed Engineering takes engineers who are experts in the core technology and embeds them directly with industry experts. They help build solutions that work for a specific context, then extract the generalizable pieces that can be reused across similar problems. Always build towards something reusable. Going to market when no market exists (5 minute read) Building a new category requires ignoring almost all standard business advice. Since nobody knows they have a problem yet, your product technically has zero value. This lures founders into a fatal pricing mistake. They lower the price to get traction, but this actually destroys the business model. You cannot sell a new idea without an expensive sales team, and a low price makes that team impossible to afford. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Win $1M @ NexHacks, the biggest hackathon in the U.S. (Sponsor) Apply to NexHacks, Jan 17th-18th at CMU, to compete for $1M in prizes from Nvidia, JPM, Cursor, Polymarket, Vercel, etc. We're also inviting 100 VCs/founders for the biggest speed-dating session, backed by a16z, General Catalyst, YC, etc. If you're a builder, don't miss out. Apps due in 2 days.																											Apply at nexhacks.com and email nexhacks@fiiai.org for sponsorship inquiries. Jeeva AI (Tool) Jeeva is an AI sales agent that automates prospecting, outreach, and follow-ups so you can focus on closing deals and building relationships. Sanctum (Tool) Ship your features to simulated users. LLMHub (Tool) LLMHub is an AI-powered collaborative search platform that turns research into shared workspaces with real-time agents and zero friction for teams. 🎁 Miscellaneous How startups lose their edge (6 minute read) In football, "prevent defense" is a strategy used to protect a lead. It usually backfires. This same mindset kills startups. When an IPO gets close, founders often stop playing to win and start playing "safe." They hire people with impressive resumes who dilute the culture and turn product building into a committee process. The moment you stop doing the weird, risky things that got you traction is the moment you cap your outcome. 192 Weeks (15 minute read) After leaving a pivotal role at Modal, a startup that grew to a $1 billion valuation, this engineer reflects on the journey from college to the tech industry. Modal offered opportunities to build complex systems, navigate social dynamics within a growing team, and contribute significantly to the codebase. Looking forward, the engineer plans to pursue new challenges while maintaining a focus on innovative system development and meaningful connections. ⚡️ Quick Links How to do user research without users (4 minute read) Suggesting you build a product without talking to humans is usually heresy in Silicon Valley. Two Ideas on AI: RL and revenue (4 minute read) Vertical SaaS is about to repeat the "Embedded Finance" success story, but this time with AI. How to use AI for SEO without just writing 'Slop' (4 minute read) Writing "undetectable" AI content is a low-ROI trap. The Alpha is Not LLM Monitoring (4 minute read) Investors just dumped $227 million into startups that essentially build fancy dashboards. title: TLDR Founders 2025-12-01
text: TLDR Founders 2025-12-01 How to sell to devs 👨‍💻, common accounting mistakes 📊, lessons from Supabase CEO 📚 📊 Startups on Carta combined to raise over $27 billion in Q3 2025 — see what's driving growth (Sponsor) Startups on Carta raised the highest amount of funding in three years — up 48% over 2023. The latest State of Private Markets report analyzes this and other trends in the venture capital industry. Read the report to learn why : Early-stage valuations are rising Down rounds are growing less frequent The Bay Area still reigns supreme Although most of the market is raising at steady levels, a few mega-checks, sometimes reaching billions of dollars, are influencing Q3 totals and valuation benchmarks. Read the full findings and analysis 📈 Headlines & Trends Supabase CEO on the "painful" decisions that built a $5B company (1 minute read) Supabase, valued at $5 billion, raised $100 million after previously raising $200 million at a $2 billion valuation. Its CEO, Paul Copplestone, prioritizes sticking to a product vision over accepting demanding enterprise contracts. What Does a "Healthy" Venture Market Actually Look Like? (4 minute read) Boutique Seed funds continue to lead the majority of sub-$10M rounds, despite mega-funds increasingly entering earlier-stage investments. Series A activity clusters around $20M, but competition intensifies above that level, with mega-funds taking a dominant role in rounds above $50M. This shift in dynamics compels investors to differentiate by emphasizing talent and innovation, as traditional stage boundaries blur and AI drives significant opportunity for reinvention. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How to sell to engineers (9 minute read) Engineers assume all salespeople are incompetent. They think about scalability, speed, and reliability, but not money so much. Focus on how your product will save time and reduce effort. Don't be too loud, don't agitate them, and let them come to you. Most Common Accounting Mistakes (7 minute read) There are a lot of accounting mistakes that can make benchmarking an apples-to-oranges comparison. Your accounting needs to be clean, or your leaders and board won't trust you. This post discusses the most common accounting mistakes. Don't wait until you are deep in M&A diligence or prepping for an IPO before correcting these mistakes, or it may materially impact metrics. Peter Thiel on how to identify great talent (2 minute read) Peter Thiel recruited the founders of YouTube, LinkedIn, Yelp, and Affirm before any of them started those companies. When asked what he looks for in undiscovered talent, he said he wants people who are both stubborn and open-minded. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Justworks: One Platform for Payroll, HR, and International Contractors (Sponsor) Scaling a team across borders? Justworks lets you pay US employees and international contractors in 60+ countries from a single dashboard - no tool-juggling required. Pay in local currency or USD, with 24/7 support when you need it. Sign up for any plan now and add on international contractors for free until 2027. Get started with Justworks. Trae (Tool) Trae is an AI IDE that collaborates like a real engineer. It plans, codes, and orchestrates your full development workflow to help you ship faster. Replit for designers (Tool) Turn any creative concept into an interactive, fully functional app with Replit. Yapify (Tool) Voice-powered email tool that drafts replies in your style using context-aware AI, saving time by turning spoken input into perfectly formatted messages. 🎁 Miscellaneous Your perfectionism is lying to you (4 minute read) We often mistake perfectionism for "high standards," but it is usually just procrastination wearing a tuxedo. The obsession with flawlessness prevents you from finishing. In creative work, completion is the only currency that matters. The most successful builders shift their goal from being "perfect" to being "prolific." You are not sculpting stone, you are planting a garden. The only way to get quality is to force yourself to ship quantity. Why Airtable bought DeepSky (5 minute read) Airtable recently acquired DeepSky just four months after launch. The speed of the deal signals a shift in what enterprises value in AI. While most startups compete on the "execution layer" (writing text or code faster), DeepSky focused on the "judgment layer" - the messy research and reasoning that happens before a decision is made. Instead of instantly answering a prompt, the system pauses to plan, runs parallel research threads, and synthesizes findings into business memos. Airtable owns the workflow. By acquiring DeepSky, it now owns the thinking that feeds it. 9-9-6-0: When all you build is character (5 minute read) The current AI gold rush is built on a dangerous gamble: trading "996" work weeks (9 am to 9 pm, 6 days a week) for a future lottery ticket. While VCs risk borrowed money, employees risk borrowed time - an asset that cannot be refunded. When the bubble inevitably bursts, many will face the "9-9-6-0" outcome: years of sacrifice that yielded zero dollars. ⚡️ Quick Links Garage studios (3 minute read) The "garage startup" is a Silicon Valley cliché, but the psychology behind it is real. The Information Diet (3 minute read) Your brain evolved to seek information for survival, but the modern world hijacks this instinct with high-calorie, low-nutrient "junk food" like 24-hour news and social outrage. Running a Business Means Contact With Reality (8 minute read) Effectively selling requires active effort and understanding customer behavior, often through simple tactics that are underestimated. Checklist for VC Due Diligence (6 minute read) VC due diligence involves evaluating a startup's founding team, market opportunity, financials, legal structure, and product. title: TLDR Founders 2025-12-03
text: TLDR Founders 2025-12-03 AI transformation 🤖, age of scaling ⚖️, investor call framework 📚 The monetization model powering Lovable's breakout growth (Sponsor) As Lovable's growth rocketed, they needed a new way to price AI. While traditional SaaS pricing models are breaking down, it's not yet clear what will take their place. In this live webinar , Elena Verna, Lovable's Head of Growth, will share pricing strategies her team uses to better reflect the value AI delivers. Join to learn : 1️⃣ Why AI has upended the logic of SaaS pricing and why there's still no clear playbook 2️⃣ How Lovable navigates the tension between self-serve and enterprise monetizations 3️⃣ What it takes to evolve from usage-based pricing to outcomes-based value Save your seat 📈 Headlines & Trends The Age of Scaling Is Over! (4 minute read) AI labs have spent five years betting that more data and more GPUs would solve everything. That bet is no longer paying off. As scaling laws flatten, the industry is shifting focus to "test-time compute", forcing models to spend more resources reasoning during a task rather than just training bigger brains. Bet the farm (3 minute read) Many companies are bragging about wide-scale AI adoption, but their hallway conversations tell a different story: many employees haven't opened AI tools in weeks. Dashboards are showing logins, not usage. This is transformation theater. Companies that go all in on AI by making it mandatory tell a different story: increased revenue and lower headcount. The best people see the theater and leave for places where transformation is real. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics 9 Reasons ICPs Don't Book A Demo (5 minute read) Most companies run ads before they actually know who they're selling to. The "GTM Delta" framework forces you to fix your foundation, “Market and Message,” before spending a dime on sales plays. If you don't know exactly who your buyer is, automation just scales your failure. Stop dumping "content libraries" on prospects. Your job isn't to be a library - it's to be a guide. The Investor Call Framework (4 minute read) The moment you lose control of the meeting structure is the moment you lose the investment. Investors judge your leadership potential based on your ability to drive a conversation, not just your slide deck. A winning 30-minute call requires a strict psychological sequence: Intro, Context, Proof, Ask, and Next Steps. Do not wait for the VC to lead the discussion. You set the agenda, you frame the problem, and you define exactly what happens after the call ends. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Why SaaS companies should own their payment process (Sponsor) If you allow an external provider to display their branding on your checkout, you're disrupting the customer journey and promoting another brand instead of your own. Learn how to avoid these common mistakes in NMI's guide to white-label payments . Want to see how embedded payments will work in your app? Get a demo (or sandbox account) RunLLM (Tool) RunLLM is an AI agent that resolves technical issues by reading logs and code. True3D banners (Tool) Turn your existing image or video creative into an interactive ad that reduces CPC by 30%. Clover Security (Tool) Design-led product security with AI agents that act like experienced security architects and extend the team's reach. 🎁 Miscellaneous The Awkward Exit: $150M to $300M Acquisitions (5 minute read) A $200M exit is often life-changing for a founder but a failure for a Series C investor. This "Grey Zone" creates boardroom deadlock. Early investors (Seed/Series A) are happy with a 5x return, while late-stage funds that underwrote a $1B outcome may block the sale to avoid a write-down. The solution: negotiate secondary sales during your growth rounds. Silicon Valley Builds Amazon and Gmail Copycats to Train AI Agents (6 minute read) Several Silicon Valley startups have spent the past several months recreating popular websites so AI systems can learn to navigate the internet and complete specific tasks on their own. If an AI system learns to use a replica of the site, it can use the real site, too. The shadow sites are a significant part of the tech industry's efforts to transform chatbots into AI agents. Many companies believe that AI agents will be able to replace some white-collar workers in the coming years. ⚡️ Quick Links Vanity Activities (2 minute read) Optimization often masks itself as productivity. A beta is like inviting guests over (2 minute read) The main advantage of a beta is not research - it is basic hygiene. Jeff Bezos on the 4 principles that differentiated Amazon (2 minute read) Amazon's edge comes from four simple habits: obsessing over customers, inventing instead of copying, thinking in 5–7 year horizons, and taking operations seriously.
