text: TLDR Founders 2026-03-25 Revenue durability crisis 💸, end of free trials 📉, finding PMF 👨‍💻 ⚡ HR shouldn't feel like a second job. (Sponsor) You didn't start your company to spend hours on payroll spreadsheets and benefits paperwork, but somehow, that's what happens when you hire your first employee. With Justworks , you can simplify all of that work. Your payroll, benefits, and compliance all live in one place, so you can actually focus on what you built your company to do. And whether you're hiring your first employee or scaling your team, Justworks handles the complexities for you with real 24/7 human support. Learn more at [justworks.com] 📈 Headlines & Trends Why free trials don't make sense anymore (and what user acquisition tactic to try instead) (11 minute read) AI-driven costs, shorter payback expectations, and web funnels are causing free trials to decline as companies push for faster monetization. Short trials now dominate despite lower conversion. Use paid trials and trial qualifier events to improve signal quality. They filter for high-intent users, enabling more effective UA optimization while maintaining sufficient event volume for ad networks. Revenue Durability is Failing in the Age of AI (7 minute read) Every VC, PE firm, and public market investor is circling the same question in 2026: Is this company's revenue actually durable? The answer is crushing valuations, freezing M&A, and making fundraising brutal. Bill.com dropped from 90% growth to 12%. Snowflake fell from 73% to 26%. The uncomfortable truth is that nobody has real confidence that software revenue will hold up over the next decade, and that uncertainty alone is enough to reshape how every founder thinks about pricing, retention, and defensibility. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics I wanted to build a vertical SaaS for pest control. I took a technician job instead (7 minute read) Taking a job in an industry you want to get into is a good way to figure out what problems the industry faces. Terry Clarke wanted to build a vertical SaaS for pest control, so he applied for jobs, got licensed, and started documenting all the issues he faced in his role. He moved about the company he worked in to learn more about how it worked, and eventually left the company to start his own. The experience helped him learn what tooling to build for his platform. How To Build a PMF Machine (9 minute read) Start with a metric, then build the feature. Rank your ideas according to which of them has the highest probability of moving the metric. Estimate how much effort each idea would take to realize. You can then see which tasks are high-impact and low effort. Constantly revise roadmaps to avoid wasting engineering time, and measure everything to calibrate better. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Stop logging CRM data. Start using it. (Sponsor) Lightfield captures every call, email, and meeting — no manual entry. Ask it anything: what objections keep coming up, which deals are going cold, what your best customers have in common. Cited answers from real conversations. Migrate from any CRM in under an hour. Get 3 months free → lightfield.app Use code TLDRS43 Littlebird (Tool) AI assistant that knows what you're working on. Remix (Tool) Turn your raw ideas into content with parallel AI agents. Tobira.ai (Tool) Network where AI agents connect and negotiate deals on behalf of users. 🎁 Miscellaneous There are only two paths left for software (11 minute read) Software companies face two paths to survive market pressure: accelerate growth through new AI-native products or achieve over 40% operating margins, including stock compensation. CEOs need to decisively select a path, restructure leadership, and either innovate with high-growth AI products or refocus on profitability through higher efficiency and pricing. Choosing neither and staying in the middle will lead to growth pressure and diminished market value. Software engineering calories: what would cal.ai say about your product? (7 minute read) Founders should treat their products like their bodies. We gain weight if we eat more calories than we burn, and lose weight if we burn more calories than we eat. For software, the input is code, and the output is feature adoption. Some products would genuinely be better if half their features were deleted. More weight should mean more strength. ⚡️ Quick Links AI didn't kill product intuition. It exposed who never had it (7 minute read) Real product intuition is knowing which ideas are fragile before investing in them. Why better signals drive paid search performance (8 minute read) Paid search performance hinges on optimizing the quality of the input signals given to bidding algorithms. How Money Flows (2 minute read) A mental model for why some startups raise millions while others get ignored - capital flows like water toward the biggest, most painful problems. What we wish we knew about building AI agents (9 minute read) Knowing these things would have helped PostHog launch months earlier, provide a better early experience to users, and make progress on capabilities faster. How to Make Something Great (2 minute read) Greatness is not about one-shotting perfection - it is the compound interest of iteration, curiosity, and caring more than anyone else. text: TLDR Founders 2026-03-27 Vertical models 🤖, AI costs 💰, the imagination chasm 🧠 The billing engine that powers Anthropic is now self-serve, with startup-friendly pricing (Sponsor) Metronome is the platform that leading companies like Anthropic and Confluent use to experiment with and scale billing. Now it's even easier for startups to get started with Metronome. Follow the link to: 1️⃣ Get a free sandbox. 2️⃣ Start building. 3️⃣ Test billing flows before committing anything. 4️⃣ Get startup-friendly pricing once you go live. Billing is a product. Make it grow with you. 📈 Headlines & Trends The age of vertical models is here (6 minute read) Intercom's Fin agent is objectively the highest performing, fastest, and cheapest model for customer service. The model resolves almost two million issues per week, a number that is growing exponentially. It resolves customer issues at a higher rate than any other model available. The model has already grown to nearly $100 million in recurring revenue in its short life. AI Companies Are Building for the Wrong Users (9 minute read) The gap between the early adoption and maturation phases is closing fast. The early adopters of AI have gotten the industry to where it is now. The next phase of growth will come from users who are curious but skeptical, willing to try but not interested enough to figure out how to work around the rough edges of the technology. This audience will determine whether AI delivers on its productivity promises. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics The Imagination Chasm: Why 99% of Useful Agents Haven't Been Built Yet (10 minute read) Most industries still can't define what an "agent" actually is. You can't build what you can't picture. This post lays out the six things still missing before agent-to-agent economies work: a communication layer (MCP/A2A), vendor-agnostic infra, dynamic auth, a payments layer, a marketplace, and real benchmarks. OpenClaw expanded imagination, but it's still the first 1%. Avoiding The Eye of Sauron (6 minute read) Founders in AI face heightened competition from incumbents, startups, foundation models, and empowered customers, risking obsolescence if their products remain at the workflow level. Companies building horizontal applications for high-agency, high-capability customers are particularly vulnerable, as these customers can easily replicate such tools. Founders must prioritize deep domain mastery, addressing human-centric problems, and developing chassis products to maintain a competitive edge. AI features are eroding your subscription app's margins — here's how to fix it (13 minute read) AI features introduce variable costs that scale with user engagement. This disrupts the near-zero marginal soft model of traditional subscription apps. The most successful businesses are designing their entire systems around the economics of usage. Teams need to manage AI the same way they manage acquisition spend or infrastructure. Consider whether the AI feature is worth the cost - the improvements from implementing AI need to be measurable. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Fake compliance just became a headline. Real enforcement has always been the answer. (Sponsor) Buyers want proof your security controls are actually working — not just a certificate that says they are. Zip Security connects your identity provider, MDM, and EDR into one enforced posture. Up and running in two weeks or less. No security hire needed. → See it in action → How Pine Park Health stays HIPAA compliant with enforced security controls Cognee (Tool) Your AI skills observe their own failures, figure out what broke, and fix themselves. Agentplace AI Agents (Tool) Create and manage task-specific AI agents for workflows. 🎁 Miscellaneous SaaS Was About Unbundling. AI Is About Rebundling (4 minute read) The SaaS playbook was simple - own one workflow, expand from there. AI companies are doing the opposite -  they're expanding into adjacent categories almost immediately because the cost of building is near zero. Models change every 42 days now, and buyers would rather trust one platform for 3-5 years than stitch together a best-of-breed stack that's outdated by next quarter. 2026 State of GTM: The Benchmarks That Actually Matter (7 minute read) Companies under $100M ARR are accelerating again, likely driven by AI-native companies growing abnormally fast. CAC payback period is the metric to watch - revenue durability is uncertain, and shorter contract lengths are becoming the norm. GTM efficiency on AI products with 30% gross margins looks very different from that of traditional software with 75% margins. ⚡️ Quick Links Storytelling as a Leadership Skill (1 minute read) Storytelling is a crucial leadership skill that ensures team alignment and clarity. Issue tracking is dead (2 minute read) The next era of product development is built on context and agency. Why Elon Musk Wins: Game Theory Applied (3 minute read) Elon Musk succeeds because he commits early in markets where GPUs, energy, capital, and manufacturing are constrained. text: TLDR Founders 2026-03-30 YC $1M ARR benchmark 📈, state of software 💻, selling to agents 🤖 Unlock the data your product can't see (Sponsor) Most tools are still locked to their own database, blind to everything users already have in Slack, GitHub, Salesforce, Google Drive, and dozens of other apps. That's the ceiling on what you can build. WorkOS Pipes removes it. One API call connects your product to the apps your users live in. Pull context from their tools, cross-reference data across silos, power AI agents that act across services. All with fresh, managed credentials you never have to think about. Start building connected products → 📈 Headlines & Trends YC W26 Demo Day: 14 Companies Hit $1M ARR Before Presenting (5 minute read) 190 companies presented this week. 85% were AI-first. 14 hit $1M ARR before demo day, the highest ever for any YC batch. The bigger signal here is that the bar for what counts as "early stage" keeps moving. $1M ARR used to be a Series A milestone. Now, it's table stakes for a demo day presentation. Reflections on the State of the Software and AI Market (9 minute read) The AI market isn't the same as the Dotcom bubble of the past. The technology is still early, and demand is pulling spending forward. The software selloff is real, but it won't hit every layer equally, as each has its own moats. Across the last few technological revolutions, the companies that became durable winners were predominantly founded in years four and five of each platform shift. We are now in that window for AI. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics Selling to AI Agents (6 minute read) Every major tech shift creates a new growth channel. AI is becoming a decision-maker - they're starting to choose products. This new growth channel is embedding products directly into the AI tools that people use. This impacts strategy, product, and engineering. The companies that will be marketing to the interface layers that make decisions on users' behalf. Per Token Pricing Is Coming for Every AI Company (8 minute read) The hardest problem in AI right now isn't the model, it's the pricing. In traditional SaaS, you could underprice a seat, and it didn't matter because the marginal cost of serving another user is basically zero. Now every agent action, every "magic" feature triggers real inference costs on the backend. Price too low and you're literally paying customers to use your product. Price too high and you lose to the competitor who figured it out first. Per-token and credit-based pricing models are about to become the default because they're the only ones that align cost structure with value delivered. ⚒️ Tools & Resources Your AI tools are powerful. They just have no idea what you're working on. (Sponsor) Littlebird observes your screen and meetings, building a private memory that grows with you. No setup, no catching it up. This is what AI was supposed to feel like. RequestClip (1 minute read) Verify Humans with Short Video. Voxtrall (Tool) Mistral open-sourced a text-to-speech model that outperforms ElevenLabs on benchmarks. Dunky AI (Tool) Practice and improve startup pitches with feedback. 🎁 Miscellaneous The Only Path Left in Software: Accelerate or Die (6 minute read) Software companies have three paths right now: accelerate revenue 10%+ YoY through AI-native products, get stuck in the messy middle, or rebuild to 40-50% operating margins. Even the profitability path is deceptive. Most companies that look profitable on FCF margins are still in the messy middle once you account for stock-based compensation. The only real option is to accelerate. If you're not rewriting your product around AI, you're choosing a slow death. Who to hire at startups: antifragile people (12 minute read) Antifragility is the premise that some things, including people, improve from stressful situations. Disorder and uncertainty are the rule at startups. Over time, antifragile people will consistently improve their value/skills due to the continued exposure to a startup's disorder. ⚡️ Quick Links The Energy Situation, Explained for Tech People (20 minute read) The Strait of Hormuz is the most critical energy dependency in the world, and it has no failover. Heidi Sturrock shares how a costly mistake became a competitive advantage (5 minute read) Heidi Sturrock's broad match campaign mistake unexpectedly became an advantage by turning competitor calls into sales leads. Digital Twins Are About to Matter for AI Agents (4 minute read) When a key employee leaves, their judgment and pattern recognition usually walk out the door with them. Digital twins are being repurposed from manufacturing (where you build a digital copy of a physical asset) to capture that kind of workflow knowledge, things like quote-to-cash, onboarding, any process that lives in someone's head, so an agent can run it. Services: The New Software (10 minute read) The next $1 trillion company will be a software company masquerading as a services firm. In 2025, the fastest-growing AI companies were copilots. This year, many will try to become autopilots. This will result in a dilemma where selling the work means cutting their own customers out of doing it. 🧠 Strategies & Tactics How Founders Get Better at Storytelling (5 minute read) Storytelling is a skill that compounds. It starts with articulating your story to yourself, then editing and clarifying it until the story becomes both simple and specific. As your story gets clearer, everything downstream gets easier. This takes time, so find opportunities to practice. What's GTM as Product? (7 minute read) Most teams treat GTM like a junk drawer of sales scripts, one-off automations, and whatever worked last quarter. Teams should treat GTM like an actual product, with memory, workflows, guardrails, and version control baked in. That's where "vibe marketing" needs to end up if it's going to last. ⚒️ Tools & Resources 85% of Unicorns Run on Salesforce. Five moats that still hold up: compounding proprietary data, network effects, regulatory permission, capital at scale, and physical infrastructure. If your moat is complexity or execution speed, it's shrinking. If it's access to something scarce, it's probably getting stronger. Trader Mindset (7 minute read) The "Trader Mindset" suggests a cultural shift towards short-term gains over long-term investment, driven by trends like crypto and day trading. This approach explains job-hopping, multiple VC investments in similar spaces, and the quick pivot from web3 to AI. While it can lead to financial success, true greatness and enriching relationships come from sustained commitment and belief. ⚡️ Quick Links The Batman trap: when helping isn't helping (10 minute read) Consultants should guide clients to solutions by understanding their actual needs, not just executing tasks.
